title: alpha: Alpha
short_def: 1. the first letter of the alphabet2. (figuratively) only3. (numerically) the first4. (prefix, commonly) not, without (a-, an-, i.e. amoral, anarchy)5. (prefix, occasionally) in the sense of a union (i.e. akin, adelphos "brother"){Often used as a prefix in composition in the sense of privation (as a contraction from G427); occasionally in the sense of union (as a contraction of G260)}
strongs_def: Alpha.Of Hebrew origin; the first letter of the alphabet; figuratively, only (from its use as a numeral) the first: --Alpha. Often used (usually an, before a vowel) also in composition (as a contraction from aneu) in the sense of privation; so, in many words, beginning with this letter; occasionally in the sense of union (as a contraction of hama).
nas_def: alpha, as num. 1 or 1000, as prefix (1) negative (2) copulative (3) intensive.

title: Aarón: Aaron
strongs_def: Aaron. Of Hebrew origin ('Aharown); Aaron, the brother of Moses -- Aaron.

title: Abaddón: Abaddon
strongs_def: Abaddon. Of Hebrew origin ('abaddown); a destroying angel -- Abaddon.

title: abarés: Not burdensome, light, unburdensome

title: Abba: Father
short_def: 1. (vocatively) father{referring to or addressing one's father}
strongs_def: Abba. Of Chaldee origin ('ab); father as a vocative -- Abba.

title: Habel: Abel

title: Abia: Abijah

origin: [from G1 (α - Alpha) (as a negative particle) and G1062 (γάμος - wedding feast)]

title: apelaunó: To drive away, to send away, to expel

title: apeleutheros: Freedman

strongs_def: freeman. From apo and eleutheros; one freed away, i.e. A freedman -- freeman.
nas_def: one freed away, i.e. a freedman

title: Apellés: Apelles
strongs_def: Apelles. Of Latin origin; Apelles, a Christian -- Apelles.

title: apelpizó: To despair, to give up hope, to expect nothing in return.

strongs_def: fully expectFrom apo and elpizo; to hope out, i.e. Fully expect -- hope for again.

strongs_def: before, contrary, over against. From apo and enanti; from in front, i.e. Opposite, before or against -- before, contrary, over against, in the presence of.

title: aperitmétos: Uncircumcised

strongs_def: uncircumcised. From a (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of peritemno; uncircumcised (figuratively) -- uncircumcised.

title: apo: from, away from, of, by

In composition (as a prefix) it usually denotes separation, departure, cessation, completion, reversal, etc.

title: harpagmos: Grasping, robbery, something to be seized
strongs_def: plunderFrom harpazo; plunder (properly concrete) -- robbery.
nas_def: the act of seizing or the thing seized

title: harpazó: To seize, snatch, take away by force
kjv: catch (away, up), pluck, pull, take (by force)
strongs_def: catch, seize, take by force. From a derivative of haireomai; to seize (in various applications) -- catch (away, up), pluck, pull, take (by force).
nas_def: to seize, catch up, snatch away
nasb: carry off (1), caught (4), snatch (2), snatched...away (1), snatches (1), snatches away (1), snatching (1), take...away...by force (1), take...by force (2).

title: harpax: Rapacious, ravenous, extortioner, robber
strongs_def: ravenousFrom harpazo; rapacious -- extortion, ravening.

title: arrabón: Pledge, Guarantee, Deposit
short_def: 1. a pledge, i.e. part of the purchase-money or property given in advance as security for the rest
strongs_def: earnest. Of Hebrew origin (arabown); a pledge, i.e. Part of the purchase-money or property given in advance as security for the rest -- earnest.
nas_def: an earnest (a part payment in advance for security)

title: arraphos: Seamless
origin: [from G1 (α - Alpha) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of the same as G4476 (ῥαφίς - needle)]
short_def: 1. unsewed, i.e. of a single piece
strongs_def: without seam, of a single pieceFrom a (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of the same as rhaphis; unsewed, i.e. Of a single piece -- without seam.

title: arrén: Male, man
strongs_def: male, man. Arsen (ar'-sane) probably from airo; male (as stronger for lifting) -- male, man.

title: arrétos: Inexpressible, unspeakable
origin: [from G1 (α - Alpha) (as a negative particle) and the same as G4490 (ῥητῶς - explicitly)]
strongs_def: unspeakable. From a (as a negative particle) and the same as rhetos; unsaid, i.e. (by implication) inexpressible -- unspeakable.

title: arróstos: Sick, ill
origin: [from G1 (α - Alpha) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4517 (ῥώννυμι - farewell)]
strongs_def: feeble, sickly. From a (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of rhonnumi; infirm -- sick (folk, -ly).

title: arsenokoites: Homosexual, sodomite
kjv: abuser of (that defile) self with mankind
strongs_def: homosexualFrom arrhen and koite; a sodomite -- abuser of (that defile) self with mankind.

title: Artemas: Artemas
origin: [contracted from a compound of G735 (Ἄρτεμις - Artemis) and G1435 (δῶρον - gifts)]
short_def: 1. gift of Artemis2. Artemas (or Artemidorus), a Christian
strongs_def: Artemas. Contracted from a compound of Artemis and doron; gift of Artemis; Artemas (or Artemidorus), a Christian -- Artemas.
nas_def: "gift of Artemis," Artemas, a friend of the apostle Paul

title: Artemis: Artemis
origin: [probably from a derivative of G737 (ἄρτι - now)]
short_def: 1. prompt2. Artemis, the name of a Grecian goddess borrowed by the Asiatics for one of their deities
strongs_def: Diana. Probably from the same as artemon; prompt; Artemis, the name of a Grecian goddess borrowed by the Asiatics for one of their deities -- Diana.
nas_def: Artemis, the name of the Gr. goddess of the hunt

title: artemón: foresail
short_def: 1. (properly) something ready2. (specially) the topsail (rather than foresail or jib) of a vessel{(more remotely from G142; something hung up)}
strongs_def: mainsail. From a derivative of arti; properly, something ready (or else more remotely from airo (compare artos); something hung up), i.e. (specially) the topsail (rather foresail or jib) of a vessel -- mainsail.

title: arti: Now, at this moment, just now
origin: [adverb from a derivative of G142 (αἴρω - take) through the idea of suspension]
kjv: this day (hour), hence(-forth), here(-after), hither(-to), (even) now, (this) present
strongs_def: this day, henceforth, hereafter, even now. Adverb from a derivative of airo (compare artos) through the idea of suspension; just now -- this day (hour), hence(-forth), here(-after), hither(-to), (even) now, (this) present.
nasb: hereafter* (1), just (1), now (30), once (1), present (1), right now (1), this time (1).

title: artigennétos: Newborn, newly born
strongs_def: new born. From arti and gennetos; just born, i.e. (figuratively) a young convert -- new born.

title: artios: Complete, perfect, fully equipped
strongs_def: perfect, completeFrom arti; fresh, i.e. (by implication) complete -- perfect.

title: artos: Bread
strongs_def: bread, loaf. From airo; bread (as raised) or a loaf -- (shew-)bread, loaf.

title: artuó: To season, to prepare, to arrange
origin: [from a presumed derivative of G142 (αἴρω - take)]
short_def: 1. to prepare, i.e. spice (with stimulating condiments)
strongs_def: to season. From a presumed derivative of airo; to prepare, i.e. Spice (with stimulating condiments) -- season.

title: Arphaxad: Arphaxad
strongs_def: Arphaxad. Of Hebrew origin ('Arpakshad); Arphaxad, a post-diluvian patriarch -- Arphaxad.
nas_def: Arphaxad, a son of Shem and an ancestor of Christ

title: archaggelos: Archangel
strongs_def: archangel. From archo and aggelos; a chief angel -- archangel.

title: archaios: Ancient, old, former

nasb: ancient (2), ancients (2), early (1), long standing (1), old (4), old things (1).

title: Archelaos: Archelaus

nas_def: "people-ruling," Archelaus, a son of Herod the Great and king of Judea, Samaria and Idumea

title: arché: Beginning, origin, ruler, authority, principality
kjv: beginning, corner, (at the, the) first (estate), magistrate, power, principality, principle, rule
short_def: 1. (properly abstract) a commencement2. (concretely) chief (in various applications of order, time, place, or rank)
strongs_def: magistrate, power, principality, principle, rule. From archomai; (properly abstract) a commencement, or (concretely) chief (in various applications of order, time, place, or rank) -- beginning, corner, (at the, the) first (estate), magistrate, power, principality, principle, rule.
nasb: beginning (38), corners (2), domain (1), elementary (1), elementary* (1), first (1), first preaching (1), principalities (1), rule (4), rulers (6).

short_def: 1. the high-priest2. (literally) of the Jews3. (typically) Christ4. (by extension) a chief priest
strongs_def: high priest, chief priest. From arche and hiereus; the high-priest (literally, of the Jews, typically, Christ); by extension a chief priest -- chief (high) priest, chief of the priests.

strongs_def: chief shepherd. From arche and poimen; a head shepherd -- chief shepherd.

title: Archippos: Archippus
strongs_def: Archippus. From arche and hippos; horse-ruler; Archippus, a Christian -- Archippus.

title: archisunagógos: Ruler of the synagogue, synagogue leader

strongs_def: master builder. From arche and tekton; a chief constructor, i.e. "architect" -- masterbuilder.

title: architelónés: Chief tax collector
origin: [from G746 (ἀρχή - beginning) and G5057 (τελώνης - tax collectors)]
strongs_def: chief among the publicans. From arche and telones; a principle tax-gatherer -- chief among the publicans.

title: aróma: Spice, aromatic substance

strongs_def: sweet spice. From airo (in the sense of sending off scent); an aromatic -- (sweet) spice.

title: Asaph: Asaph
strongs_def: Asa. Of Hebrew origin ('Aca'); Asa, an Israelite -- Asa.

strongs_def: without life. From a (as a negative particle) and psuche; lifeless, i.e. Inanimate (mechanical) -- without life.

title: Baal: Baal
short_def: 1. Baal, a Phoenician deity2. (literally) master{used as a symbol of idolatry}
strongs_def: Baal. Of Hebrew origin (Ba'al); Baal, a Phoenician deity (used as a symbol of idolatry) -- Baal.

title: Babulón: Babylon
short_def: 1. Babylon, the capitol of Chaldaea{literally or figuratively (as a type of tyranny)}

title: bathmos: Step, Degree
strongs_def: degree. From the same as bathos; a step, i.e. (figuratively) grade (of dignity) -- degree.

title: bathos: Depth, deep, profundity

strongs_def: deepness, depth. From the same as bathus; profundity, i.e. (by implication) extent; (figuratively) mystery -- deep(-ness, things), depth.

strongs_def: deep. From bathus; to deepen -- deep.

strongs_def: deepFrom the base of basis; profound (as going down), literally or figuratively -- deep, very early.

title: baion: Palm branch

short_def: 1. a palm twig (as going out far)
strongs_def: palm branchA diminutive of a derivative probably of the base of basis; a palm twig (as going out far) -- branch.

title: Balaam: Balaam

strongs_def: Balaam. Of Hebrew origin (Bil'am); Balaam, a Mesopotamian (symbolic of a false teacher) -- Balaam.

strongs_def: BalakOf Hebrew origin (Balaq); Balak, a Moabite -- Balac.

title: balló: To throw, cast, put, place
kjv: arise, cast (out), dung, lay, lie, pour, put (up), send, strike, throw (down), thrust
short_def: 1. to throw{in various applications, more or less violent or intense}
strongs_def: cast out, send, throw down, thrust A primary verb; to throw (in various applications, more or less violent or intense) -- arise, cast (out), dung, lay, lie, pour, put (up), send, strike, throw (down), thrust. Compare rhipto.
nasb: bring (2), cast (15), casting (3), casts (3), contributors (1), laid (1), lying (3), lying sick (1), place (1), poured (4), put (22), puts (2), putting (4), rushed (1), swung (2), threw (12), throw (16), thrown (27), tossing (1).

title: baptizó: To baptize, to immerse, to dip
short_def: 1. to immerse, submerge2. to make whelmed or soaked (i.e. fully wet){used only (in the New Testament) of ceremonial ablution, especially (technically) of the ordinance of Christian baptism}
strongs_def: baptize, wash. From a derivative of bapto; to immerse, submerge; to make whelmed (i.e. Fully wet); used only (in the New Testament) of ceremonial ablution, especially (technically) of the ordinance of Christian baptism -- Baptist, baptize, wash.
nasb: Baptist (3), baptize (9), baptized (51), baptizes (1), baptizing (10), ceremonially washed (1), undergo (1).

short_def: 1. (literally) to dip (in water), i.e. cover completely with a fluid2. (in the New Testament only in a qualified or special sense) to moisten (a part of one's person)3. (by implication) to stain (as with dye)

title: desmos: Bond, chain, fetter, imprisonment
origin: [neuter and masculine respectively from G1210 (δέω - bound)]
short_def: 1. a band, i.e. ligament (of the body) or shackle (of a prisoner)2. (figuratively) an impediment or disability
strongs_def: ligament, chain, string. Or desmos des-mos' neuter and masculine respectively from deo; a band, i.e. Ligament (of the body) or shackle (of a prisoner); figuratively, an impediment or disability -- band, bond, chain, string.

title: desmophulax: Jailer, Prison Keeper
strongs_def: jailor, keeper of the prison. From desmon and phulax; a jailer (as guarding the prisoners) -- jailor, keeper of the prison.

title: desmótérion: Prison, jail
origin: [from a derivative of G1199 (δεσμόν - imprisonment) (equivalent to G1196 (δεσμέω - To bind))]
short_def: 1. a place of bondage, i.e. a dungeon
strongs_def: prison. From a derivative of desmon (equivalent to desmeo); a place of bondage, i.e. A dungeon -- prison.

title: desmótés: Prisoner, captive
origin: [from a derivative of G1199 (δεσμόν - imprisonment) (equivalent to G1196 (δεσμέω - To bind))]
strongs_def: prisoner. From the same as desmoterion; (passively) a captive -- prisoner.

title: despotés: Master, Lord, Sovereign
origin: [perhaps from G1210 (δέω - bound) and posis (a husband)]
strongs_def: Lord, master. Perhaps from deo and posis (a husband); an absolute ruler ("despot") -- Lord, master.

title: deuro: "Come," "come here," "hither"
short_def: 1. here{used also imperative hither!; and of time, hitherto}
strongs_def: come hither.Of uncertain affinity; here; used also imperative hither!; and of time, hitherto -- come (hither), hither(-to).

title: deute: "Come," "Come here," "Come now"
origin: [from G1204 (δεῦρο - come) and an imperative form of eimi "to go"]
strongs_def: come, follow. From deuro and an imperative form of eimi (to go); come hither! -- come, follow.

title: deuteraios: Second day
strongs_def: next day. From deuteros; secondary, i.e. (specially) on the second day -- next day.

title: deina: certain one, such a one
short_def: 1. second-first2. (specially) a designation of the Sabbath immediately after the Paschal week (being the second after Passover day, and the first of the seven Sabbaths intervening before Pentecost)
strongs_def: second, after the first. From deuteros and protos; second-first, i.e. (specially) a designation of the Sabbath immediately after the Paschal week (being the second after Passover day, and the first of the seven Sabbaths intervening before Pentecost) -- second... After the first.

title: deuteros: Second
short_def: 1. second (in time, place, or rank)2. (also adverb) secondly
strongs_def: afterward, again, secondarily. As the compare of duo; (ordinal) second (in time, place, or rank; also adverb) -- afterward, again, second(-arily, time).
nasb: second (30), second one (2), second time (10), subsequently (1), twice (1).

title: dechomai: To receive, accept, welcome
short_def: 1. to receive{(in various applications, literally or figuratively)}
strongs_def: accept, receive, take. Middle voice of a primary verb; to receive (in various applications, literally or figuratively) -- accept, receive, take. Compare lambano.
nasb: accept (2), accepted (3), receive (16), received (12), receives (15), take (3), taken (1), took (1), welcome (2), welcomed (1).

title: deó: To bind, tie, fasten
kjv: bind, be in bonds, knit, tie, wind
short_def: 1. to bind2. (regarding marriage) to be bethrothed, to pledge to give oneself in marriage{in various applications, literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: bind, be in bonds, knit, tie, wind. A primary verb; to bind (in various applications, literally or figuratively) -- bind, be in bonds, knit, tie, wind. See also dei, deomai.
nasb: bind (7), binding (1), binds (2), bound (23), imprisoned (4), prisoners (1), put...in chains (1), tied (4).

title: dé: but, and, now, moreover
origin: [probably akin to G1161 (δέ - now), a particle of emphasis or explicitness]
strongs_def: also, and, doubtless, now, therefore. Probably akin to de; a particle of emphasis or explicitness; now, then, etc. -- also, and, doubtless, now, therefore.
nas_def: indeed, now (used to give emphasis or urgency to a statement)

title: délos: Clear, evident, manifest
strongs_def: certain, evident, manifest.Of uncertain derivation; clear -- + bewray, certain, evident, manifest.

title: déloó: To make clear, to show, to declare, to reveal.
strongs_def: declare, show, signify. From delos; to make plain (by words) -- declare, shew, signify.
nasb: denotes (1), indicating (1), informed (2), made clear (1), show (1), signifying (1).

title: Démas: Demas
strongs_def: Demas. Probably for Demetrios; Demas, a Christian -- Demas.

title: démégoreó: To address a public assembly, to make a public speech
origin: [from a compound of G1218 (δῆμος - assembly) and G58 (ἀγορά - market places)]
short_def: 1. to be a people-gatherer, i.e. to address a public assembly
strongs_def: make an oration. From a compound of demos and agora; to be a people-gatherer, i.e. To address a public assembly -- make an oration.

title: Démétrios: Demetrius
short_def: 1. Demetrius, the name of an Ephesian and of a Christian
strongs_def: Demetrius. From Demeter (Ceres); Demetrius, the name of an Ephesian and of a Christian -- Demetrius.
nas_def: Demetrius, the name of a silversmith and of a Christian

title: démiourgos: Creator, Craftsman, Maker
short_def: 1. a worker for the people, i.e. mechanic (spoken of the Creator)
strongs_def: maker. From demos and ergon; a worker for the people, i.e. Mechanic (spoken of the Creator) -- maker.

title: démos: People, populace, public
strongs_def: people. From deo; the public (as bound together socially) -- people.

title: démosios: Public, belonging to the people
short_def: 1. public2. (feminine singular dative case as adverb) in public
strongs_def: common, open, public. From demos; public; (feminine singular dative case as adverb) in public -- common, openly, publickly.

title: dénarion: Denarius
strongs_def: denarius, pence, penny. Of Latin origin; a denarius (or ten asses) -- pence, penny(-worth).

title: dépote: at any time, ever
origin: [from G1211 (δή - indeed) and G4218 (ποτέ - formerly), a particle of generalization]
strongs_def: whatsoever. From de and pote; a particle of generalization; indeed, at any time -- (what-)soever.

title: dépou: Surely, indeed, perhaps
origin: [from G1211 (δή - indeed) and G4225 (πού - somewhere), a particle of asseveration]
strongs_def: verily. From de and pou; a particle of asseveration; indeed doubtless -- verily.

title: dia: through, by, because of, for the sake of
origin: [a primary preposition denoting the channel of an act]
kjv: after, always, among, at, to avoid, because of (that), briefly, by, for (cause) fore, from, in, by occasion of, of, by reason of, for sake, that, thereby, therefore, though, through(-out), to, wherefore, with (-in) In composition it retains the same general importance
short_def: 1. through{in very wide applications, local, causal, or occasional}
strongs_def: through, on account ofA primary preposition denoting the channel of an act; through (in very wide applications, local, causal, or occasional) -- after, always, among, at, to avoid, because of (that), briefly, by, for (cause)... Fore, from, in, by occasion of, of, by reason of, for sake, that, thereby, therefore, though, through(-out), to, wherefore, with (-in). In composition it retains the same general importance.
nas_def: through, on account of, because of
nasb: account (4), after (2), afterward (1), always* (2), because (111), between* (1), briefly* (1), charge* (1), constantly (1), continually* (6), during (1), forever* (1), gives (1), means (3), over (1), presence (1), reason (40), sake (41), sakes (5), since (1), so then* (1), so* (1), therefore* (16), this reason* (1), this* (1), though (1), through (225), through the agency (1), through* (1), view (2), way (3), what (1), why (3), why* (27).

title: diabainó: To cross over, to pass through, to go across
origin: [from G1223 (διά - through) and the base of G939 (βάσις - feet)]
strongs_def: come over, pass through. From dia and the base of basis; to cross -- come over, pass (through).

title: diaballó: To slander, accuse, bring charges against
strongs_def: accuse. From dia and ballo; (figuratively) to traduce -- accuse.

title: diabebaioomai: To affirm strongly, to assert confidently, to declare emphatically.

short_def: 1. to confirm thoroughly (by words), i.e. asseverate
strongs_def: affirm constantly. Middle voice of a compound of dia and bebaioo; to confirm thoroughly (by words), i.e. Asseverate -- affirm constantly.

title: diablepó: To see clearly, to look intently
short_def: 1. to look through, i.e. recover full vision

title: diabolos: Devil, accuser, slanderer
strongs_def: false accuser, devil, slanderer. From diaballo; a traducer; specially, Satan (compare satan) -- false accuser, devil, slanderer.

title: diaggelló: To proclaim thoroughly, to declare, to announce
origin: [from G1223 (διά - through) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος - Angel)]
strongs_def: declare, preach, signify. From dia and the base of aggelos; to herald thoroughly -- declare, preach, signify.

strongs_def: enquire, know the uttermost. From dia and ginosko; to know thoroughly, i.e. Ascertain exactly -- (would) enquire, know the uttermost.

title: diagnórizó: To distinguish, to discern, to make known thoroughly
origin: [from G1123 (γραπτός - written) and G1107 (γνωρίζω - made known)]
strongs_def: make known. From graptos and gnorizo; to tell abroad -- make known.

title: diagogguzó: To murmur, to grumble, to complain
strongs_def: murmur. From dia and gogguzo; to complain throughout a crowd -- murmur.

strongs_def: come after. From dia and dechomai; to receive in turn, i.e. (figuratively) succeed to -- come after.

title: diadéma: Diadem, Crown
origin: [from a compound of G1223 (διά - through) and G1210 (δέω - bound)]
short_def: 1. a "diadem" (as bound about the head){simpler and thinner than G4735}
strongs_def: crown. From a compound of dia and deo; a "diadem" (as bound about the head) -- crown. Compare stephanos.

short_def: 1. to give throughout a crowd, i.e. deal out2. also to deliver over (as to a successor)
strongs_def: distribute, divide, give. From dia and didomi; to give throughout a crowd, i.e. Deal out; also to deliver over (as to a successor) -- (make) distribute(-ion), divide, give.

title: diadochos: Successor, one who follows or takes over
strongs_def: successorFrom diadechomai; a successor in office -- room.

title: diazónnumi: To gird, to fasten, to prepare
strongs_def: gird, put onFrom dia and zonnumi; to gird tightly -- gird.

title: diathéké: Covenant, Testament
short_def: 1. (properly) a disposition2. (specially) a contract (especially a devisory will)
strongs_def: covenant, testament. From diatithemai; properly, a disposition, i.e. (specially) a contract (especially a devisory will) -- covenant, testament.

strongs_def: administration, office, service. From diakonos; attendance (as a servant, etc.); figuratively (eleemosynary) aid, (official) service (especially of the Christian teacher, or technically of the diaconate) -- (ad-)minister(-ing, -tration, -try), office, relief, service(-ing).

strongs_def: deacon, minister, servant. Probably from an obsolete diako (to run on errands; compare dioko); an attendant, i.e. (genitive case) a waiter (at table or in other menial duties); specially, a Christian teacher and pastor (technically, a deacon or deaconess) -- deacon, minister, servant.

title: diakosioi: Two hundred

title: diakouó: To hear thoroughly, to listen intently

From dia and krino; to separate thoroughly, i.e. (literally and reflexively) to withdraw from, or (by implication) oppose; figuratively, to discriminate (by implication, decide), or (reflexively) hesitate -- contend, make (to) differ(-ence), discern, doubt, judge, be partial, stagger, waver.

title: exastraptó: To flash like lightning, to gleam, to shine brightly
short_def: 1. to lighten forth2. (figuratively) to be radiant (of very white garments)
strongs_def: glistening. From ek and astrapto; to lighten forth, i.e. (figuratively) to be radiant (of very white garments) -- glistening.
nas_def: to flash or gleam like lightning, be radiant

title: exautés: Immediately, at once, forthwith
origin: [from G1537 (ἐκ - among) and the genitive case singular feminine of G846 (αὐτός - himself) (G5610 (ὥρα - hour) being understood)]
kjv: by and by, immediately, presently, straightway
strongs_def: by and by, immediately, presentlyFrom ek and the genitive case singular feminine of autos (hora being understood); from that hour, i.e. Instantly -- by and by, immediately, presently, straightway.

title: exegeiró: To awaken, to raise up
short_def: 1. to rouse fully2. (figuratively) to resuscitate (from death), release (from infliction)
strongs_def: raise up. From ek and egeiro; to rouse fully, i.e. (figuratively) to resuscitate (from death), release (from infliction) -- raise up.

title: exeimi: To go out, to depart
short_def: 1. to go out, go forth2. to leave (a place)3. to escape (to the shore)
strongs_def: depart, get to land, go out. From ek and eimi (to go); to issue, i.e. Leave (a place), escape (to the shore) -- depart, get (to land), go out.

title: exelegchó: To expose, to convict, to refute, to reprove
short_def: 1. to convict fully2. (by implication) to punish
strongs_def: convince. From ek and elegcho; to convict fully, i.e. (by implication) to punish -- convince.

title: exelkó: To draw out, to drag out
short_def: 1. to drag forth2. (figuratively) to entice (to sin)
strongs_def: draw away. From ek and helkuo; to drag forth, i.e. (figuratively) to entice (to sin) -- draw away.

title: exerama: Vomit
origin: [from a comparative of G1537 (ἐκ - among) and a presumed erao "to spew"]
strongs_def: vomit. From a comparative of ek and a presumed erao (to spue); vomit, i.e. Food disgorged -- vomit.

title: exeraunaó: To search out, to examine thoroughly
strongs_def: search diligently. From ek and ereunao; to explore (figuratively) -- search diligently.

title: exerchomai: To go out, to come out, to depart
kjv: come (forth, out), depart (out of), escape, get out, go (abroad, away, forth, out, thence), proceed (forth), spread abroad
strongs_def: come forth, depart, escape, get outFrom ek and erchomai; to issue (literally or figuratively) -- come (forth, out), depart (out of), escape, get out, go (abroad, away, forth, out, thence), proceed (forth), spread abroad.
nas_def: to go or come out of
nasb: came (33), came forth (4), come (22), come forth (3), comes (1), coming (7), departed (1), departing (1), descended* (1), eluded (1), get (2), go (21), go away (2), go* (1), goes (2), going (2), gone (13), gone forth (2), got (2), leave (4), left (15), proceeded forth (1), spread (3), went (61), went ashore (2), went away (3), went forth (5), went off (1).

title: exesti: It is lawful, it is permitted, it is possible.
origin: [third person singular present indicative of a compound of G1537 (ἐκ - among) and G1510 (εἰμί - am), so also exon ex-on' neuter present participle of the same (with or without some form of G1510 (εἰμί - am) expressed)]
short_def: 1. impersonally, it is right (through the figurative idea of being out in public)
strongs_def: so also Third person singular present indicative of a compound of ek and eimi; so also exon ex-on' neuter present participle of the same (with or without some form of eimi expressed); impersonally, it is right (through the figurative idea of being out in public) -- be lawful, let, may(-est).

title: exetazó: To examine, to investigate, to inquire thoroughly
short_def: 1. to test thoroughly (by questions), i.e. ascertain or interrogate
strongs_def: ask, enquire, search. From ek and etazo (to examine); to test thoroughly (by questions), i.e. Ascertain or interrogate -- ask, enquire, search.

title: exégeomai: To explain, to interpret, to declare
short_def: 1. to consider out (aloud), i.e. rehearse, unfold
strongs_def: declare, tell. From ek and hegeomai; to consider out (aloud), i.e. Rehearse, unfold -- declare, tell.

title: hexékonta: Sixty
strongs_def: sixty, threescore. The tenth multiple of hex; sixty -- sixty(-fold), threescore.

title: hexés: Next, following, in order, subsequently
origin: [from G2192 (ἔχω - have) (in the sense of taking hold of, i.e. adjoining)]
strongs_def: after, following, tomorrow, next. From echo (in the sense of taking hold of, i.e. Adjoining); successive -- after, following, morrow, next.

title: exécheó: To sound forth, to echo, to resound
origin: [middle voice from G1537 (ἐκ - among) and G2278 (ἠχέω - noisy)]
short_def: 1. to "echo" forth, i.e. resound (be generally reported)
strongs_def: sound forth. Middle voice from ek and echeo; to "echo" forth, i.e. Resound (be generally reported) -- sound forth.

title: hexis: Habit, practice, condition
strongs_def: habit, practice, useFrom echo; habit, i.e. (by implication) practice -- use.

title: existémi: To amaze, to astonish, to be beside oneself, to be out of one's mind
kjv: amaze, be (make) astonished, be beside self (selves), bewitch, wonder
short_def: 1. to put (stand) out of wits, i.e. astound2. (reflexively) become astounded, insane
strongs_def: amaze, make astonished, bewitch, wonder. From ek and histemi; to put (stand) out of wits, i.e. Astound, or (reflexively) become astounded, insane -- amaze, be (make) astonished, be beside self (selves), bewitch, wonder.
nas_def: to displace, to stand aside from
nasb: amazed (10), amazement (1), astonished (2), astonishing (1), astounded (1), beside ourselves (1), lost His senses (1).

title: exischuó: To prevail, to have strength, to be able
short_def: 1. to have full strength, i.e. be entirely competent
strongs_def: be able. From ek and ischuo; to have full strength, i.e. Be entirely competent -- be able.

title: exodos: Departure, Exodus, Way Out
strongs_def: a departure, exodusFrom ek and hodos; an exit, i.e. (figuratively) death -- decease, departing.

title: exolethreuó: To utterly destroy, to exterminate, to root out
origin: [from G1537 (ἐκ - among) and G3645 (ὀλοθρεύω - To destroy)]
strongs_def: destroy. From ek and olothreuo; to extirpate -- destroy.

title: exomologeó: To confess, to acknowledge, to give thanks
short_def: 1. to acknowledge or (by implication, of assent) agree fully
strongs_def: confess, profess, promise. From ek and homologeo; to acknowledge or (by implication, of assent) agree fully -- confess, profess, promise.
nasb: confess (2), confessed (1), confessing (2), consented (1), give praise (2), praise (2).

title: exorkizó: To adjure, to charge under oath, to solemnly command
short_def: 1. to exact an oath, i.e. conjure
strongs_def: adjure. From ek and horkizo; to exact an oath, i.e. Conjure -- adjure.
nas_def: to administer an oath, to adjure

title: exorkistés: Exorcist
short_def: 1. one that binds by an oath (or spell)2. (by implication) an "exorcist" (conjurer)
strongs_def: exorcist. From exorkizo; one that binds by an oath (or spell), i.e. (by implication) an "exorcist" (conjurer) -- exorcist.

title: exorussó: To dig out, to gouge out
short_def: 1. to dig out2. (by extension) to extract (an eye), remove (roofing)
strongs_def: extract, pluck out. From ek and orusso; to dig out, i.e. (by extension) to extract (an eye), remove (roofing) -- break up, pluck out.

title: exoudeneó: To despise, to treat with contempt, to regard as nothing
origin: [from G1537 (ἐκ - among) and a derivative of the neuter of G3762 (οὐδείς - no one)]
short_def: 1. to make utterly nothing of, i.e. despise
strongs_def: to despise, treat with contemptFrom ek and a derivative of the neuter of oudeis; to make utterly nothing of, i.e. Despise -- set at nought. See also exoutheneo.

title: exoutheneó: To despise, to treat with contempt, to regard as nothing.
origin: [a variation of G1847 (ἐξουδενόω - treated with contempt) and meaning the same]
strongs_def: to despiseA variation of exoudenoo and meaning the same -- contemptible, despise, least esteemed, set at nought.
nasb: contemptible (1), despise (3), despised (1), no account (1), regard with contempt (1), regard...with contempt (1), rejected (1), treating...with contempt (1), viewed with contempt (1).

title: exousia: Authority, power, right, jurisdiction
origin: [from G1832 (ἔξεστι - lawful) (in the sense of ability)]
kjv: authority, jurisdiction, liberty, power, right, strength
short_def: 1. privilege2. (subjectively) force, capacity, competency, freedom3. (objectively) mastery (concretely, magistrate, superhuman, potentate, token of control), delegated influence
strongs_def: authority, jurisdiction, liberty, powerFrom exesti (in the sense of ability); privilege, i.e. (subjectively) force, capacity, competency, freedom, or (objectively) mastery (concretely, magistrate, superhuman, potentate, token of control), delegated influence -- authority, jurisdiction, liberty, power, right, strength.
nasb: authorities (7), authority (65), charge (1), control (1), domain (2), dominion (1), jurisdiction (1), liberty (1), power (11), powers (1), right (11).

title: exousiazó: To exercise authority, to have power over, to control
kjv: exercise authority upon, bring under the (have) power of
strongs_def: exercise authority upon, bring under the power of. From exousia; to control -- exercise authority upon, bring under the (have) power of.

title: exoché: Preeminence, prominence, excellence
origin: [from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ - among) and G2192 (ἔχω - have) (meaning to stand out)]
strongs_def: principal. From a compound of ek and echo (meaning to stand out); prominence (figuratively) -- principal.

title: exupnizó: To awaken, to rouse from sleep
strongs_def: awake out of sleep. From exupnos; to waken -- awake out of sleep.

title: exupnos: Awake, roused from sleep
strongs_def: out of sleep. From ek and hupnos; awake -- X out of sleep.

title: exó: Outside, without, out
strongs_def: away, forth, without, strange. Adverb from ek; out(-side, of doors), literally or figuratively -- away, forth, (with-)out (of, -ward), strange.
nasb: away (2), foreign (1), forth (1), outer (1), outside (20), outsiders* (3).

title: exóthen: outside, from without, outwardly
strongs_def: outside, outward, from without. From exo; external(-ly) -- out(-side, -ward, - wardly), (from) without.

title: exótheó: To drive out, to thrust out, to push away
strongs_def: drive out, propel. Or exotho ex-o'-tho from ek and otheo (to push); to expel; by implication, to propel -- drive out, thrust in.

title: exóteros: Outer, outside
strongs_def: outer. Comparative of exo; exterior -- outer.

title: heortazo: To celebrate a feast, to keep a festival
strongs_def: keep the feast. From heorte; to observe a festival -- keep the feast.

title: heorté: Feast, Festival
strongs_def: feast, holy day. Of uncertain affinity; a festival -- feast, holyday.

title: epaggelia: Promise
short_def: 1. an announcement (for information, assent or pledge)2. (especially) a divine assurance of good
strongs_def: message, promise. From epaggello; an announcement (for information, assent or pledge; especially a divine assurance of good) -- message, promise.
nasb: promise (37), promised (1), promises (12), what was promised (2).

title: epaggellomai: To promise, to proclaim, to announce
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and the base of G32 (ἄγγελος - Angel)]
short_def: 1. to announce upon (reflexively)2. (by implication) to engage to do something, to assert something respecting oneself
strongs_def: profess, promise. From epi and the base of aggelos; to announce upon (reflexively), i.e. (by implication) to engage to do something, to assert something respecting oneself -- profess, (make) promise.
nasb: made (1), made the promise (1), making a claim (1), professed (1), promise had been made (1), promised (9), promising (1).

title: epaggelma: Promise, profession
short_def: 1. a self-committal (by assurance of conferring some good)
strongs_def: promise. From epaggello; a self-committal (by assurance of conferring some good) -- promise.

title: epagó: To bring upon, to lead upon, to introduce
short_def: 1. to superinduce, i.e. inflict (an evil), charge (a crime)
strongs_def: bring upon. From epi and ago; to superinduce, i.e. Inflict (an evil), charge (a crime) -- bring upon.

title: epagónizomai: To contend earnestly, to struggle for
strongs_def: earnestly contendFrom epi and agonizomai; to struggle for -- earnestly contend foreign

title: epathroizó: To gather together, to assemble
strongs_def: accumulateFrom epi and athroizo (to assemble); to accumulate -- gather thick together.

title: Epainetos: Epainetus
strongs_def: Epenetus. From epaineo; praised; Epaenetus, a Christian -- Epenetus.

title: epaineó: To praise, to commend
strongs_def: commend, laud, praise. From epi and aineo; to applaud -- commend, laud, praise.

title: epainos: Praise, commendation, approval
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and the base of G134 (αἰνέω - praising)]
strongs_def: praise. From epi and the base of aineo; laudation; concretely, a commendable thing -- praise.

title: epairó: To lift up, to raise, to exalt
strongs_def: exalt self, poise, lift up. From epi and airo; to raise up (literally or figuratively) -- exalt self, poise (lift, take) up.
nasb: exalts (1), hoisting (1), lift (3), lifted (4), lifting (4), raised (5), turning (1).

title: epaischunomai: To be ashamed, to feel shame
strongs_def: be ashamed. From epi and aischunomai; to feel shame for something -- be ashamed.

title: epaiteó: To beg, to ask for alms
strongs_def: beg. From epi and aiteo; to ask for -- beg.

title: epakoloutheó: To follow closely, to accompany, to attend
strongs_def: follow after. From epi and akoloutheo; to accompany -- follow (after).
nasb: devoted herself (1), follow (1), follow after (1), followed (1).

title: epakouó: To hear, to listen to, to hearken
strongs_def: hear. From epi and akouo; to hearken (favorably) to -- hear.
nas_def: to listen to, to hearken to

title: epakroaomai: To listen intently, to heed, to pay close attention
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and the base of G202 (ἀκροατής - hearer)]
strongs_def: listen intently toFrom epi and the base of akroates; to listen (intently) to -- hear.

title: epan: when, whenever
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G302 (ἄν - if), a particle of indefinite contemporaneousness]
strongs_def: when. From epi and an; a particle of indefinite contemporaneousness; whenever, as soon as -- when.

title: epanagkes: Necessary, Compulsory
origin: [neuter of a presumed compound of G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G318 (ἀναγκή - compulsion)]
strongs_def: necessary. Neuter of a presumed compound of epi and anagke; (adverbially) on necessity, i.e. Necessarily -- necessary.

title: epanagó: To bring back, to return, to lead up again
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G321 (ἀνάγω - set sail)]
short_def: 1. to lead up on2. (technically) to put out (to sea)3. (intransitively) to return
strongs_def: put out to sea, thrust out, return. From epi and anago; to lead up on, i.e. (technical) to put out (to sea); (intransitively) to return -- launch (thrust) out, return.
nas_def: to put out (to sea), to return

title: epanamimnéskó: To remind again, to bring to remembrance
strongs_def: put in mind. From epi and anamimnesko; to remind of -- put in mind.

title: epanapauó: To rest upon, to settle upon, to rely on
origin: [middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G373 (ἀναπαύω - refreshed)]
strongs_def: rest upon. Middle voice from epi and anapano; to settle on; literally (remain) or figuratively (rely) -- rest in (upon).

title: epanerchomai: To return, to come back
short_def: 1. to come up on, i.e. return
strongs_def: come again, return. From epi and anerchomai; to come up on, i.e. Return -- come again, return.

title: epanistémi: To rise up against, to rebel, to stand up
origin: [middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G450 (ἀνίστημι - got)]
short_def: 1. to stand up on2. (figuratively) to attack
strongs_def: rise up against. Middle voice from epi and anistemi; to stand up on, i.e. (figuratively) to attack -- rise up against.

title: epanorthósis: Correction, improvement, reformation
origin: [from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G461 (ἀνορθόω - made erect again)]
strongs_def: correction. From a compound of epi and anorthoo; a straightening up again, i.e. (figuratively) rectification (reformation) -- correction.

title: epanó: Above, over, on top of
short_def: 1. up above, i.e. over or on (of place, amount, rank, etc.)
strongs_def: above, more than, upon, over. From epi and ano; up above, i.e. Over or on (of place, amount, rank, etc.) -- above, more than, (up-)on, over.

title: eparkeo: To help, to aid, to assist, to be sufficient for
strongs_def: relieve, assistFrom epi and arkeo; to avail for, i.e. Help -- relieve.

title: eparchia: Province
origin: [from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G757 (ἄρχω - began) (meaning a governor of a district, "eparch")]
short_def: 1. a special region of government, i.e. a Roman prefecture
strongs_def: province. From a compound of epi and archo (meaning a governor of a district, "eparch"); a special region of government, i.e. A Roman pr?Fecture -- province.

title: epaulis: Dwelling, habitation, homestead
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and an equivalent of G833 (αὐλή - courtyard)]
short_def: 1. a hut over the head, i.e. a dwelling
strongs_def: habitation, dwelling, residenceFrom epi and an equivalent of aule; a hut over the head, i.e. A dwelling -- habitation.

title: epaurion: The next day, on the morrow
short_def: 1. occurring on the succeeding day, i.e. to-morrow{(G2250 being implied)}
strongs_def: day following, tomorrow, next dayFrom epi and aurion; occurring on the succeeding day, i.e. (hemera being implied) to-morrow -- day following, morrow, next day (after).

title: epautophóró: Caught in the act
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G846 (αὐτός - himself) and (the dative case singular of) a derivative of phor (a thief)]
short_def: 1. in theft itself2. (by analogy) in actual crime
strongs_def: in the very act. From epi and autos and (the dative case singular of) a derivative of phor (a thief); in theft itself, i.e. (by analogy) in actual crime -- in the very act.

title: Epaphras: Epaphras
strongs_def: Epaphras. Contracted from Epaphroditos; Epaphras, a Christian -- Epaphras.

title: epaphrizó: To foam up, to froth
short_def: 1. to foam upon2. (figuratively) to exhibit (a vile passion)
strongs_def: foam out. From epi and aphrizo; to foam upon, i.e. (figuratively) to exhibit (a vile passion) -- foam out.

title: Epaphroditos: Epaphroditus
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) (in the sense of devoted to) and Aphrodite (Venus)]
strongs_def: Epaphroditus. From epi (in the sense of devoted to) and Aphrodite (Venus); Epaphroditus, a Christian -- Epaphroditus. Compare Epaphras.

title: epegeiró: To stir up, to awaken, to arouse
short_def: 1. to rouse upon2. (figuratively) to excite against
strongs_def: raise, stir up. From epi and egeiro; to rouse upon, i.e. (figuratively) to excite against -- raise, stir up.

title: epei: Since, because, for, then
kjv: because, else, for that (then, -asmuch as), otherwise, seeing that, since, when
short_def: 1. thereupon, i.e. since (of time or cause)
strongs_def: because, else, for that, otherwiseFrom epi and ei; thereupon, i.e. Since (of time or cause) -- because, else, for that (then, -asmuch as), otherwise, seeing that, since, when.

title: epeidé: Since, because, when
kjv: after that, because, for (that, -asmuch as), seeing, since
short_def: 1. since now2. (of time) when3. (of cause) whereas
strongs_def: after that, because, for, seeing, since. From epei and de; since now, i.e. (of time) when, or (of cause) whereas -- after that, because, for (that, -asmuch as), seeing, since.

title: epeidéper: "Inasmuch as," "since indeed," "because"
strongs_def: inasmuch. From epeide and per; since indeed (of cause) -- forasmuch.

title: epeidon: To look upon, to regard, to consider
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G1492 (εἴδω - To see)]
strongs_def: behold, look upon. And other moods and persons of the same tense; from epi and eido; to regard (favorably or otherwise) -- behold, look upon.

title: epeiper: "since indeed," "because indeed"
strongs_def: seeing. From epei and per; since indeed (of cause) -- seeing.

title: epeisagoge: Introduction, bringing in
origin: [from a compound of G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G1521 (εἰσάγω - brought)]
strongs_def: bringing in. From a compound of epi and eisago; a superintroduction -- bringing in.

title: epeita: then, thereafter, afterwards
strongs_def: afterward, then. From epi and eita; thereafter -- after that(-ward), then.

title: epekeina: Beyond
origin: [from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and (the accusative case plural neuter of) G1565 (ἐκεῖνος - those)]
short_def: 1. upon those parts of, i.e. on the further side of
strongs_def: beyond. From epi and (the accusative case plural neuter of) ekeinos; upon those parts of, i.e. On the further side of -- beyond.

title: epekteinó: To stretch out, to extend, to reach forth
origin: [middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G1614 (ἐκτείνω - stretched)]
strongs_def: reach forth. Middle voice from epi and ekteino; to stretch (oneself) forward upon -- reach forth.
nas_def: to extend, mid. to stretch forward

title: ependuomai: To be clothed upon, to put on over
origin: [middle voice from G1909 (ἐπί - over) and G1746 (ἐνδύω - To put on)]
strongs_def: be clothed upon. Middle voice from epi and enduo; to invest upon oneself -- be clothed upon.

title: ependutés: Outer garment, coat, tunic
strongs_def: fisher's coat. From ependuomai; a wrapper, i.e. Outer garment -- fisher's coat.

title: eperchomai: To come upon, to overtake, to approach
short_def: 1. to supervene, i.e. arrive, occur, impend, attack, (figuratively) influence
strongs_def: come in, come upon. From epi and erchomai; to supervene, i.e. Arrive, occur, impend, attack, (figuratively) influence -- come (in, upon).

title: eperótaó: To ask, inquire, question
short_def: 1. to ask for, i.e. inquire, seek
strongs_def: ask questions, demand, desire. From epi and erotao; to ask for, i.e. Inquire, seek -- ask (after, questions), demand, desire, question.
nasb: ask (5), ask...question (1), ask...questions (1), asked (14), asked...a question (1), asking (2), asking...questions (1), question (3), questioned (18), questioning (9).

title: eperótéma: Inquiry, question, appeal, pledge
strongs_def: an inquiryFrom eperotao; an inquiry -- answer.

title: epechó: To hold upon, to hold fast, to pay attention to, to give heed to.
kjv: give (take) heed unto, hold forth, mark, stay
short_def: 1. to hold upon2. (by implication) to retain3. (by extension) to detain4. (with implication) to pay attention to{with implication, G3563 is implied}
strongs_def: take heed unto, hold forth, mark, stay. From epi and echo; to hold upon, i.e. (by implication) to retain; (by extension) to detain; (with implication, of nous) to pay attention to -- give (take) heed unto, hold forth, mark, stay.
nas_def: to hold fast, to hold toward, to stop
nasb: give...attention (1), holding fast (1), noticed (1), pay close attention (1), stayed (1).

title: epéreazó: To insult, to mistreat, to revile, to abuse
origin: [from a comparative of G1909 (ἐπί - over) and (probably) areia "threats"]
strongs_def: mistreat, falsely accuse. From a comparative of epi and (probably) areia (threats); to insult, slander -- use despitefully, falsely accuse.

title: epi: on, upon, over, at, by, before, across, against, among, beside, in, on the basis of
kjv: about (the times), above, after, against, among, as long as (touching), at, beside, have charge of, (be-, (where-))fore, in (a place, as much as, the time of, -to), (because) of, (up-)on (behalf of), over, (by, for) the space of, through(-out), (un-)to(-ward), with
short_def: 1. (properly) meaning superimposition (of time, place, order, etc.)2. (genitive case) as a relation of distribution (over, upon, etc.)3. (dative case) of rest (at, on, etc.)4. (accusative case) of direction (towards, upon, etc.){literally or figuratively; In compounds it retains essentially the same import (at, upon, etc.)}

strongs_def: Cleopas. Probably contracted from Kleopatros (compound of kleos and pater); Cleopas, a Christian -- Cleopas.

title: kleos: Glory, Renown, Fame
origin: [from a shorter form of G2564 (καλέω - called)]
strongs_def: glory. From a shorter form of kaleo; renown (as if being called) -- glory.

title: kleptés: Thief

strongs_def: thief. From klepto; a stealer (literally or figuratively) -- thief. Compare leistes.

title: kleptó: To steal
strongs_def: steal. A primary verb; to filch -- steal.

title: kléma: Branch
short_def: 1. a limb or shoot (as if broken off)
strongs_def: branch. From klao; a limb or shoot (as if broken off) -- branch.

title: Klémés: Clement
strongs_def: Clement. Of Latin origin; merciful; Clemes (i.e. Clemens), a Christian -- Clement.

title: kléronomeó: To inherit, to receive an inheritance, to be an heir
short_def: 1. to be an heir to{literally or figuratively}

title: kléronomos: Heir

title: kléros: Lot, portion, inheritance, share

short_def: 1. a die (for drawing chances)2. (by implication) a portion (as if so secured)3. (by extension) an acquisition (especially a patrimony, figuratively)
strongs_def: heritage, inheritance, lot, part. Probably from klao (through the idea of using bits of wood, etc., for the purpose; a die (for drawing chances); by implication, a portion (as if so secured); by extension, an acquisition (especially a patrimony, figuratively) -- heritage, inheritance, lot, part.
nasb: allotted to your charge (1), inheritance (2), lot (1), lots (5), portion (1), share (1).

strongs_def: assign or obtain an inheritance. From kleros; to allot, i.e. (figuratively) to assign (a privilege) -- obtain an inheritance.

strongs_def: after, backside, behind. From opis (regard; from optanomai) with enclitic of source; from the rear (as a secure aspect), i.e. At the back (adverb and preposition of place or time) -- after, backside, behind.

title: opisó: Behind, after
short_def: 1. to the back, i.e. aback{as adverb or preposition of time or place; or as noun}
strongs_def: after, backward, behindFrom the same as opisthen with enclitic of direction; to the back, i.e. Aback (as adverb or preposition of time or place; or as noun) -- after, back(-ward), (+ get) behind, + follow.
nasb: after (18), around (1), back (2), back* (3), behind (5), follow (2), follow* (2), withdrew* (1).

title: hoplizó: To arm, to equip
short_def: 1. to equip (with weapons (middle voice and figuratively))
strongs_def: arm self. From hoplon; to equip (with weapons (middle voice and figuratively)) -- arm self.

title: hoplon: Weapon, instrument, tool
origin: [probably from a primary hepo "to be busy about"]
short_def: 1. an implement or utensil or tool2. (especially) offensive tool for war{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: armor, instrument, weapon. Probably from a primary hepo (to be busy about); an implement or utensil or tool (literally or figuratively, especially, offensive for war) -- armour, instrument, weapon.

title: hopoios: Of what sort, what kind, such as
kjv: what manner (sort) of, such as whatsoever
short_def: 1. of what kind that, i.e. how (as) great (excellent){specially, as an indefinite correlative to the definite antecedent G5108 of quality}
strongs_def: what manner ofFrom hos and poios; of what kind that, i.e. How (as) great (excellent) (specially, as an indefinite correlative to the definite antecedent toioutos of quality) -- what manner (sort) of, such as whatsoever.
nasb: quality (1), what (1), what kind (2), what kind of person (1).

title: hopote: whenever, as often as
short_def: 1. what(-ever) then2. (of time) as soon as
strongs_def: when. From hos and pote; what(-ever) then, i.e. (of time) as soon as -- when.

title: hopou: where, wherever
kjv: in what place, where(-as, -soever), whither (+ soever)

strongs_def: in what place, whereFrom hos and pou; what(-ever) where, i.e. At whichever spot -- in what place, where(-as, -soever), whither (+ soever).

title: optanomai: to appear, to be seen, to be visible
origin: [prolonged (middle voice) form of the primary (middle voice) optomai op'-tom-ahee which is used for it in certain tenses and both as alternate of G3708 (ὁράω - saw)]
short_def: 1. to gaze (i.e. with wide-open eyes){as at something remarkable; and thus differing from G991, which denotes simply voluntary observation; and from G1492, which expresses merely mechanical, passive or casual vision; while G2300, and still more emphatically its intensive G2334, signifies an earnest but more continued inspection; and G4648 a watching from a distance)}
strongs_def: appear, look, seeA (middle voice) prolonged form of the primary (middle voice) optomai (op'-tom-ahee); which is used for it in certain tenses; and both as alternate of horao; to gaze (i.e. With wide-open eyes, as at something remarkable; and thus differing from blepo, which denotes simply voluntary observation; and from eido, which expresses merely mechanical, passive or casual vision; while theaomai, and still more emphatically its intensive theoreo, signifies an earnest but more continued inspection; and skopeo a watching from a distance) -- appear, look, see, shew self.

title: optasia: Vision, appearance
origin: [from a presumed derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι - appearing)]
strongs_def: vision. From a presumed derivative of optanomai; visuality, i.e. (concretely) an apparition -- vision.

title: optos: Roasted, cooked
origin: [from an obsolete verb akin to hepso "to steep"]
strongs_def: broiled. From an obsolete verb akin to hepso (to "steep"); cooked, i.e. Roasted -- broiled.

title: opóra: Harvest, late summer, autumn

strongs_def: fruit. Apparently from the base of opse and hora; properly, even-tide of the (summer) season (dog-days), i.e. (by implication) ripe fruit -- fruit.

title: hopós: "so that," "in order that," "how"

title: horama: Vision
short_def: 1. something gazed at, i.e. a spectacle (especially supernatural)
strongs_def: sight, vision. From horao; something gazed at, i.e. A spectacle (especially supernatural) -- sight, vision.

title: horasis: Vision, sight
short_def: 1. the act of gazing2. (externally) an aspect3. (internally) an inspired appearance
strongs_def: sight, vision. From horao; the act of gazing, i.e. (externally) an aspect or (internally) an inspired appearance -- sight, vision.

title: horatos: Visible, seen
short_def: 1. gazed at2. (by implication) capable of being seen
strongs_def: visible. From horao; gazed at, i.e. (by implication) capable of being seen -- visible.

strongs_def: anger, indignation, vengeance, wrath. From oregomai; properly, desire (as a reaching forth or excitement of the mind), i.e. (by analogy), violent passion (ire, or (justifiable) abhorrence); by implication punishment -- anger, indignation, vengeance, wrath.

title: orgizó: To be angry, to provoke to anger
short_def: 1. to provoke or enrage2. (passively) become exasperated

strongs_def: a fathom. From oregomai; a stretch of the arms, i.e. A fathom -- fathom.

title: oregó: To reach out, to stretch oneself, to desire, to aspire

title: oreinos: Mountainous, hilly

strongs_def: hill country. From oros; mountainous, i.e. (feminine by implication, of chora) the Highlands (of Judaea) -- hill country.

title: orexis: Desire, longing, appetite
short_def: 1. excitement of the mind, i.e. longing after
strongs_def: lust. From oregomai; excitement of the mind, i.e. Longing after -- lust.

title: orthopodeó: To walk uprightly, to walk straight

short_def: 1. to be straight-footed2. (figuratively) to go directly forward
strongs_def: walk uprightly. From a compound of orthos and pous; to be straight-footed, i.e. (figuratively) to go directly forward -- walk uprightly.

title: orthos: Straight, upright, correct

short_def: 1. upright (as rising)2. (perpendicularly) erect3. (figuratively) honest4. (horizontally) level or direct
strongs_def: straight, upright. Probably from the base of oros; right (as rising), i.e. (perpendicularly) erect (figuratively, honest), or (horizontally) level or direct -- straight, upright.

title: orthotomeó: To rightly divide, to cut straight

strongs_def: rightly divide. From a compound of orthos and the base of tomoteros, to make a straight cut, i.e. (figuratively) to dissect (expound) correctly (the divine message) -- rightly divide.

title: horizó: To determine, appoint, designate, or set boundaries.

strongs_def: oath. From herkos (a fence; perhaps akin to horion); a limit, i.e. (sacred) restraint (specially, an oath) -- oath.

title: pneó: To blow, to breathe
strongs_def: blow. A primary word; to breathe hard, i.e. Breeze -- blow. Compare psucho.

title: pnigó: To choke, to strangle, to suffocate
short_def: 1. to wheeze (causative)2. (by implication) to throttle or strangle (drown)
strongs_def: choke, take by the throat. Strengthened from pneo; to wheeze, i.e. (causative, by implication) to throttle or strangle (drown) -- choke, take by the throat.

title: pniktos: Strangled
short_def: 1. throttled2. (neuter concretely) an animal choked to death (not bled)
strongs_def: strangled. From pnigo; throttled, i.e. (neuter concretely) an animal choked to death (not bled) -- strangled.

title: pnoé: Breath, wind
strongs_def: breath, wind. From pneo; respiration, a breeze -- breath, wind.

title: podérés: Reaching to the feet, full-length, long robe
origin: [from G4228 (πούς - feet) and another element of uncertain affinity]
short_def: 1. a dress reaching the ankles{G2066 implied}
strongs_def: long robe, dressFrom pous and another element of uncertain affinity; a dress (esthes implied) reaching the ankles -- garment down to the foot.
nasb: robe reaching (1), robe reaching to the feet (1).

title: pothen: From where, whence
origin: [from the base of G4213 (πόσις - drink) with enclitic adverb of origin]
short_def: 1. from which (as interrogative) or what (as relative) place, state, source or cause
strongs_def: whence. From the base of posis with enclitic adverb of origin; from which (as interrogative) or what (as relative) place, state, source or cause -- whence.
nasb: how (3), what (1), what...source (1), what sense (1), where (22).

title: poiéma: Workmanship, creation, work
short_def: 1. a product, i.e. fabric{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: workmanship. From poieo; a product, i.e. Fabric (literally or figuratively) -- thing that is made, workmanship.

title: poiésis: Making, doing, creation, workmanship
strongs_def: deed. From poieo; action, i.e. Performance (of the law) -- deed.

title: poiétés: Doer, Maker, Poet
strongs_def: poet, doer From poieo; a performer; specially, a "poet"; --doer, poet.

title: poikilos: Various, manifold, diverse
strongs_def: diverse, manifold. Of uncertain derivation; motley, i.e. Various in character -- divers, manifold.

title: poimainó: To shepherd, to tend, to feed, to guide
short_def: 1. to tend as a shepherd of2. (figuratively) supervisor
strongs_def: to shepherd, ruleFrom poimen; to tend as a shepherd of (figuratively, superviser) -- feed (cattle), rule.

title: poimén: Shepherd
strongs_def: shepherd, pastor. Of uncertain affinity; a shepherd (literally or figuratively) -- shepherd, pastor.

title: poimné: flock
strongs_def: flock, fold. Contraction from poimaino; a flock (literally or figuratively) -- flock, fold.

title: poimnion: flock
origin: [neuter of a presumed derivative of G4167 (ποίμνη - flock)]
strongs_def: flock. Neuter of a presumed derivative of poimne; a flock, i.e. (figuratively) group (of believers) -- flock.

title: poios: What, which, of what kind
origin: [from the base of G4226 (ποῦ - where) and G3634 (οἷος - such)]
short_def: 1. individualizing interrogative (of character) what sort of2. (of number) which one
strongs_def: what manner of, which. From the base of pou and hoios; individualizing interrogative (of character) what sort of, or (of number) which one -- what (manner of), which.
nasb: any (1), kind of...by which (1), what (21), what kind (6), what things (1), which (3), which ones (1).

title: polemeó: To wage war, to fight, to engage in battle
short_def: 1. to be (engaged) in warfare, i.e. to battle{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: to battle, make war. From polemos; to be (engaged) in warfare, i.e. To battle (literally or figuratively): -fight, (make) war.
nasb: make war (1), quarrel (1), wage war (2), waged (1), wages war (1), waging (1).

title: polemos: War, battle, conflict
short_def: 1. warfare (a single encounter or a series){literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: battle, fight, war. From pelomai (to bustle); warfare (literally or figuratively; a single encounter or a series) -- battle, fight, war.

title: polis: City
origin: [probably from pelomai "to bustle", or perhaps from G4183 (πολύς - many)]
short_def: 1. a town (properly, with walls, of greater or less size)
strongs_def: city. Probably from the same as polemos, or perhaps from polus; a town (properly, with walls, of greater or less size) -- city.

title: politarchés: City official, ruler of a city
strongs_def: ruler of the city. From polis and archo; a town-officer, i.e. Magistrate -- ruler of the city.

title: politeia: Citizenship, commonwealth, state, way of life
strongs_def: citizenshipFrom polites ("polity"); citizenship; concretely, a community -- commonwealth, freedom.

title: politeuma: Citizenship, commonwealth
strongs_def: citizenship, community, conversation. From politeuomai; a community, i.e. (abstractly) citizenship (figuratively) -- conversation.

title: politeuomai: to live as a citizen, to conduct oneself
origin: [middle voice of a derivative of G4177 (πολίτης - citizens)]
strongs_def: let conversation be, live. Middle voice of a derivative of polites; to behave as a citizen (figuratively) -- let conversation be, live.

title: polités: Citizen
strongs_def: citizen. From polis; a townsman -- citizen.

title: pollakis: Often, frequently, many times

nasb: frequent (1), many (1), often (15), time after time (1).

title: pollaplasión: Many times more, manifold more
origin: [from G4183 (πολύς - many) and probably a derivative of G4120 (πλέκω - twisting)]

strongs_def: manifold more. From polus and probably a derivative of pleko; manifold, i.e. (neuter as noun) very much more -- manifold more.
nasb: many times (2), many times as much (2).

title: polulogia: Much speaking, verbosity
origin: [from a compound of G4183 (πολύς - many) and G3056 (λόγος - word)]
strongs_def: wordinessFrom a compound of polus and logos; loquacity, i.e. Prolixity -- much speaking.

title: polumerós: In many portions, in many parts, in various ways
origin: [adverb from a compound of G4183 (πολύς - many) and G3313 (μέρος - part)]
short_def: 1. in many portions, i.e. variously as to time and agency (piecemeal)
strongs_def: variously, at sundry times. Adverb from a compound of polus and meros; in many portions, i.e. Variously as to time and agency (piecemeal) -- at sundry times.

title: polupoikilos: Manifold, multifaceted, diverse
strongs_def: manifold. From polus and poikilos; much variegated, i.e. Multifarious -- manifold.

kjv: costly, very precious, of great price

title: polutropós: In many ways, in various manners
origin: [adverb from a compound of G4183 (πολύς - many) and G5158 (τρόπος - way)]
short_def: 1. in many ways, i.e. variously as to method or form
strongs_def: in many waysAdverb from a compound of polus and tropos; in many ways, i.e. Variously as to method or form -- in divers manners.

title: poma: Drink, beverage
strongs_def: drink. From the alternate of pino; a beverage -- drink.

title: ponéria: Wickedness, evil, malice
strongs_def: iniquity, wickedness. From poneros; depravity, i.e. (specially), malice; plural (concretely) plots, sins -- iniquity, wickedness.

title: ponéros: Evil, wicked, bad, malicious
kjv: bad, evil, grievous, harm, lewd, malicious, wicked(-ness)
short_def: 1. hurtful, i.e. evil2. (figuratively) calamitous3. (passively) ill, i.e. diseased4. (especially, morally) culpable, i.e. derelict, vicious, atrociously wicked5. (neuter, singular) mischief, malice6. (neuter plural) guilt7. (masculine, singular) the devil8. (plural) sinners{(properly) in effect or influence, and thus differing from G2556, which refers rather to essential character, as well as from G4550, which indicates degeneracy from original virtue}

title: pragma: Matter, thing, affair, business, deed

strongs_def: business, matter, thing, work. From prasso; a deed; by implication, an affair; by extension, an object (material) -- business, matter, thing, work.
nasb: anything* (1), case (1), deed (1), matter (3), thing (1), things (4).

title: pragmateia: Business, occupation, transaction
strongs_def: negotiation, transactionFrom pragmateuomai; a transaction, i.e. Negotiation -- affair.

title: pragmateuomai: To do business, to trade, to occupy
short_def: 1. to busy oneself with, i.e. to trade
strongs_def: trade, do businessFrom pragma; to busy oneself with, i.e. To trade -- occupy.

title: praitórion: Praetorium, governor's headquarters, palace
short_def: 1. the pretorium or governor's courtroom (sometimes including the whole edifice and camp)
strongs_def: courtroom, hall of judgment, palace, praetorium. Of Latin origin; the praetorium or governor's courtroom (sometimes including the whole edifice and camp) -- (common, judgment) hall (of judgment), palace, praetorium.
nas_def: Praetorium (official residence of a governor), praetorian guard

title: praktór: Officer, executor, avenger
strongs_def: officer. From a derivative of prasso; a practiser, i.e. (specially), an official collector -- officer.

title: praxis: Deed, action, practice

strongs_def: deed, office, work. From prasso; practice, i.e. (concretely) an act; by extension, a function -- deed, office, work.

title: praos: Meek, gentle, humble
origin: [a form of G4239 (πραΰς - Meek), used in certain parts]
strongs_def: meek. A form of praus, used in certain parts; gentle, i.e. Humble -- meek.

title: praotés: Gentleness, meekness
strongs_def: meekness. From praios; gentleness, by implication, humility -- meekness.

title: prasia: Group, Row, Division
origin: [perhaps from prason (a leek, and so an onion-patch)]

strongs_def: a groupPerhaps from prason (a leek, and so an onion-patch); a garden plot, i.e. (by implication, of regular beds) a row (repeated in plural by Hebraism, to indicate an arrangement) -- in ranks.

title: prassó: To practice, to do, to perform, to accomplish
kjv: commit, deeds, do, exact, keep, require, use arts
short_def: 1. to practice, i.e. perform repeatedly or habitually2. (by implication) to execute, accomplish, etc.3. (specially) to collect (dues), fare (personally){differing from G4160, which properly refers to a single act}
strongs_def: commit, do, performA primary verb; to "practise", i.e. Perform repeatedly or habitually (thus differing from poieo, which properly refers to a single act); by implication, to execute, accomplish, etc.; specially, to collect (dues), fare (personally) -- commit, deeds, do, exact, keep, require, use arts.
nasb: act (1), acted (1), attend (1), collect (1), collected (1), committed (3), deeds* (1), do (7), does (1), doing (2), done (6), performing (1), practice (9), practiced (2), practices (1), practicing (1).

title: praus: Meek, gentle, humble
strongs_def: meek. Apparently a primary word; mild, i.e. (by implication) humble -- meek. See also praios.

title: prautés: Gentleness, meekness
strongs_def: meekness. From praus; mildness, i.e. (by implication) humility -- meekness.

title: prepó: to be fitting, to be proper, to be suitable
short_def: 1. to tower up (be conspicuous)2. (by implication) to be suitable or proper3. (third person singular present indicative, often used impersonally) it is fit or right
strongs_def: comely. Apparently a primary verb; to tower up (be conspicuous), i.e. (by implication) to be suitable or proper (third person singular present indicative, often used impersonally, it is fit or right) -- become, comely.

strongs_def: eldest, old. Comparative of presbus (elderly); older; as noun, a senior; specially, an Israelite Sanhedrist (also figuratively, member of the celestial council) or Christian "presbyter" -- elder(-est), old.

strongs_def: Priscilla. Diminutive of Priska; Priscilla (i.e. Little Prisca), a Christian woman -- Priscilla.

nasb: before (1), bring (1), bring...forward (1), brought (1), brought...before (1), former (1), get (1), go ahead (4), goes too far (1), going ahead (3), going before (1), led the way (1), previously made (1), walking on ahead (1), went in front (1), went on before (1).

title: pros: to, towards, with, for
origin: [a strengthened form of G4253 (πρό - before), a preposition of direction]
kjv: about, according to, against, among, at, because of, before, between, (where-)by, for, at thy house, in, for intent, nigh unto, of, which pertain to, that, to (the end that), together, to (you) -ward, unto, with(-in) In the comparative case, it denotes essentially the same applications, namely, motion towards, accession to, or nearness at
short_def: 1. forward to, i.e. toward2. (genitive case) the side of, i.e. pertaining to3. (dative case) by the side of, i.e. near to4. (accusative case, usually) the place, time, occasion, or respect (which is the destination of the relation, i.e. whither or for which it is predicated)
strongs_def: to, towardsA strengthened form of pro; a preposition of direction; forward to, i.e. Toward (with the genitive case, the side of, i.e. Pertaining to; with the dative case, by the side of, i.e. Near to; usually with the accusative case, the place, time, occasion, or respect, which is the destination of the relation, i.e. Whither or for which it is predicated) -- about, according to, against, among, at, because of, before, between, (where-)by, for, at thy house, in, for intent, nigh unto, of, which pertain to, that, to (the end that), together, to (you) -ward, unto, with(-in). In the comparative case, it denotes essentially the same applications, namely, motion towards, accession to, or nearness at.
nas_def: advantageous for, at (denotes local proximity), toward (denotes motion toward a place)

strongs_def: purpose, set forth. Middle voice from pro and tithemi; to place before, i.e. (for oneself) to exhibit; (to oneself) to propose (determine) -- purpose, set forth.

title: protrepó: To urge, to encourage, to incite, to persuade

short_def: 1. to turn forward for oneself, i.e. encourage
strongs_def: exhort. Middle voice from pro and the base of trope; to turn forward for oneself, i.e. Encourage -- exhort.

title: protrechó: To run ahead, to outrun
origin: [from G4253 (πρό - before) and G5143 (τρέχω - ran) (including its alternate)]
short_def: 1. to run forward, i.e. outstrip, precede
strongs_def: outrun, run before. From pro and trecho (including its alternate); to run forward, i.e. Outstrip, precede -- outrun, run before.

title: prouparchó: To exist previously, to pre-exist
short_def: 1. to exist before2. (adverbially) to be or do something previously
strongs_def: exist beforehand From pro and huparcho; to exist before, i.e. (adverbially) to be or do something previously -- + be before(-time).

title: prophasis: Pretext, excuse, pretense
origin: [from a compound of G4253 (πρό - before) and G5316 (φαίνω - appeared)]
strongs_def: pretense, outward showingFrom a compound of pro and phaino; an outward showing, i.e. Pretext -- cloke, colour, pretence, show.

title: propheró: To bring forth, to offer, to present
strongs_def: bring forth. From pro and phero; to bear forward, i.e. Produce -- bring forth.

title: prophéteia: Prophecy

nasb: prophecies (1), prophecy (15), prophesying (1), prophetic utterance (1), prophetic utterances (1).

title: prophéteuó: To prophesy, to speak forth by divine inspiration, to predict
short_def: 1. to foretell events, divine, speak under inspiration, exercise the prophetic office
strongs_def: prophesy. From prophetes; to foretell events, divine, speak under inspiration, exercise the prophetic office -- prophesy.

title: prophétés: Prophet
origin: [from a compound of G4253 (πρό - before) and G5346 (φημί - said)]
short_def: 1. a foreteller ("prophet")2. (by analogy) an inspired speaker3. (by extension) a poet
strongs_def: prophet. From a compound of pro and phemi; a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet -- prophet.
nas_def: a prophet (an interpreter or forth-teller of the divine will)

title: prophétikos: Prophetic
strongs_def: propheticFrom prophetes; pertaining to a foreteller ("prophetic") -- of prophecy, of the prophets.

title: prophétis: Prophetess
short_def: 1. a female foreteller or an inspired woman
strongs_def: prophetess. Feminine of prophetes; a female foreteller or an inspired woman -- prophetess.

title: prophthanó: To anticipate, to come before, to precede
short_def: 1. to get an earlier start of, i.e. anticipate
strongs_def: anticipate, prevent. From pro and phthano; to get an earlier start of, i.e. Anticipate -- prevent.

title: procheirizó: To appoint, to designate, to choose
origin: [middle voice from G4253 (πρό - before) and a derivative of G5495 (χείρ - hands)]
short_def: 1. to handle for oneself in advance2. (figuratively) to purpose

title: procheirotoneó: To appoint, to choose, to elect
strongs_def: choose before. From pro and cheirotoneo; to elect in advance -- choose before.

title: Prochoros: Prochorus
short_def: 1. before the dance2. Prochorus, a Christian
strongs_def: Prochorus. From pro and choros; before the dance; Prochorus, a Christian -- Prochorus.

title: prumna: Stern
strongs_def: stern, hinder part Feminine of prumnus (hindmost); the stern of a ship -- hinder part, stern.

title: prói: Early, early in the morning
short_def: 1. at dawn2. (by implication) the day-break watch
strongs_def: early, morning. Adverb from pro; at dawn; by implication, the day-break watch -- early (in the morning), (in the) morning.

title: próia: Morning, early morning
origin: [feminine of a derivative of G4404 (πρωΐ - morning) as noun]
strongs_def: early, morning. Feminine of a derivative of proi as noun; day-dawn -- early, morning.

title: próimos: Early
short_def: 1. dawning2. (by analogy) autumnal (showering, the first of the rainy season)
strongs_def: early rain, early crops. From proi; dawning, i.e. (by analogy) autumnal (showering, the first of the rainy season) -- early.

title: próinos: Early, morning
short_def: 1. pertaining to the dawn, i.e. matutinal
strongs_def: morning. From proi; pertaining to the dawn, i.e. Matutinal -- morning.

title: próra: Bow, prow (of a ship)
origin: [feminine of a presumed derivative of G4253 (πρό - before) as noun]
short_def: 1. the prow, i.e. forward part of a vessel
strongs_def: forepartFeminine of a presumed derivative of pro as noun; the prow, i.e. Forward part of a vessel -- forepart(-ship).

title: próteuó: To have the first place, to be preeminent
short_def: 1. to be first (in rank or influence)
strongs_def: have preeminence. From protos; to be first (in rank or influence) -- have the preeminence.

title: prótokathedria: Chief seat, place of honor
short_def: 1. a sitting first (in the front row), i.e. preeminence in council
strongs_def: chief seatFrom protos and kathedra; a sitting first (in the front row), i.e. Preeminence in council -- chief (highest, uppermost) seat.

title: prótoklisia: Place of honor, chief seat
short_def: 1. a reclining first (in the place of honor) at the dinner-bed, i.e. preeminence at meals
strongs_def: chief place, seat of honorFrom protos and klisia; a reclining first (in the place of honor) at the dinner-bed, i.e. Preeminence at meals -- chief (highest, uppermost) room.
nasb: place of honor (2), places of honor (3).

title: próton: First, firstly, before, at the beginning
origin: [neuter of G4413 (πρῶτος - first) as adverb (with or without G3588 (ὁ - those))]
kjv: before, at the beginning, chiefly (at, at the) first (of all)
short_def: 1. firstly (in time, place, order, or importance)
strongs_def: before, at the beginningNeuter of protos as adverb (with or without ho); firstly (in time, place, order, or importance) -- before, at the beginning, chiefly (at, at the) first (of all).

title: prótos: First, foremost, chief, principal
kjv: before, beginning, best, chief(-est), first (of all), former
short_def: 1. foremost (in time, place, order or importance)
strongs_def: first, beginning, best, chiefContracted superlative of pro; foremost (in time, place, order or importance) -- before, beginning, best, chief(-est), first (of all), former.
nasb: before (3), best (1), first (128), first of all (2), first importance (1), first man (1), first one (1), first things (1), first time (1), foremost (5), leading (2), leading man (1), leading men (5), outer (3), previous (1).

title: prótostatés: Leader, Chief, Principal
short_def: 1. one standing first in the ranks, i.e. a captain (champion)
strongs_def: ringleader. From protos and histemi; one standing first in the ranks, i.e. A captain (champion) -- ringleader.
nas_def: one who stands first (of soldiers), hence a leader

title: prótotokia: Birthright
strongs_def: birthright. From prototokos; primogeniture (as a privilege) -- birthright.

title: prototokos: Firstborn
origin: [from G4413 (πρῶτος - first) and the alternate of G5088 (τίκτω - gave birth)]
short_def: 1. first-born{usually as noun; literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: firstbornFrom protos and the alternate of tikto; first-born (usually as noun, literally or figuratively) -- firstbegotten(-born).

title: ptaió: To stumble, to fall, to err, to sin
short_def: 1. to trip2. (figuratively) to err, sin, fail (of salvation)
strongs_def: offend, cause to stumble. A form of pipto; to trip, i.e. (figuratively) to err, sin, fail (of salvation) -- fall, offend, stumble.

title: pterna: Heel
strongs_def: heel. Of uncertain derivation; the heel (figuratively) -- heel.

title: pterugion: Pinnacle, wing, edge
origin: [neuter of a presumed derivative of G4420 (πτέρυξ - wings)]
strongs_def: pinnacle. Neuter of a presumed derivative of pterux; a winglet, i.e. (figuratively) extremity (top corner) -- pinnacle.
