title: pterux: Wing
origin: [from a derivative of G4072 (πέτομαι - flying) (meaning a feather)]
strongs_def: wing. From a derivative of petomai (meaning a feather); a wing -- wing.

title: pténos: Feathered, winged
strongs_def: bird. Contraction for peteinon; a bird -- bird.

title: ptoeó: To frighten, to terrify, to be alarmed
origin: [probably akin to the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω - fell) (through the idea of causing to fall) or to G4072 (πέτομαι - flying) (through that of causing to fly away)]
strongs_def: frighten. Probably akin to the alternate of pipto (through the idea of causing to fall) or to petomai (through that of causing to fly away); to scare -- frighten.

title: ptoésis: Fear, Terror, Alarm
strongs_def: amazement. From ptoeo; alarm -- amazement.

title: Ptolemais: Ptolemais
origin: [from Ptolemaios (Ptolemy, after whom it was named)]
strongs_def: Ptolemais. From Ptolemaios (Ptolemy, after whom it was named); Ptolemais, a place in Palestine -- Ptolemais.
nas_def: Ptolemais, a seaport south of Tyre

title: ptuon: Winnowing fork, fan
strongs_def: fan. From ptuo; a winnowing-fork (as scattering like spittle) -- fan.

origin: [from a presumed derivative of G4429 (πτύω - spitting) (and thus akin to G4422 (πτοέω - startled))]

short_def: 1. to fold, i.e. furl a scroll
strongs_def: close. Probably akin to petannumi (to spread; and thus apparently allied to petomai through the idea of expansion, and to ptuo through that of flattening; compare pateo); to fold, i.e. Furl a scroll -- close.

title: ptuó: To spit
strongs_def: spit. A primary verb (compare ptusso); to spit -- spit.

title: ptóma: Corpse, dead body, carcass
strongs_def: dead body, carcass, corpse. From the alternate of pipto; a ruin, i.e. (specially), lifeless body (corpse, carrion) -- dead body, carcase, corpse.
nas_def: a fall, hence a misfortune, ruin

title: ptósis: Fall, downfall, ruin
short_def: 1. a crash, i.e. downfall{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: fall. From the alternate of pipto; a crash, i.e. Downfall (literally or figuratively) -- fall.

title: ptócheia: Poverty
strongs_def: poverty. From ptocheuo; beggary, i.e. Indigence (literally or figuratively) -- poverty.

strongs_def: become poor. From ptochos; to be a beggar, i.e. (by implication) to become indigent (figuratively) -- become poor.
nas_def: to be a beggar, be destitute

title: ptóchos: Poor, destitute, beggar
origin: [from ptosso "to crouch", akin to G4422 (πτοέω - startled) and the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω - fell)]
short_def: 1. a beggar (as cringing), a pauper2. (figuratively) distressed3. (literally, as a noun) strictly denoting absolute or public dependency on charity{also used in a qualified or relative sense; whereas G3993 properly means only financial distress in private}
strongs_def: beggar, poor. From ptosso (to crouch); akin to ptoeo and the alternate of pipto); a beggar (as cringing), i.e. Pauper (strictly denoting absolute or public mendicancy, although also used in a qualified or relative sense; whereas penes properly means only straitened circumstances in private), literally (often as noun) or figuratively (distressed) -- beggar(-ly), poor.
nas_def: (of one who crouches and cowers, hence) beggarly, poor

title: pugmé: Fist, a measure of length from the elbow to the knuckles.

strongs_def: fistFrom a primary pux (the fist as a weapon); the clenched hand, i.e. (only in dative case as adverb) with the fist (hard scrubbing) -- oft.

title: puthón: Python, spirit of divination
origin: [from Putho (the name of the region where Delphi, the seat of the famous oracle, was located)]

strongs_def: divination. From Putho (the name of the region where Delphi, the seat of the famous oracle, was located); a Python, i.e. (by analogy, with the supposed diviner there) inspiration (soothsaying) -- divination.

title: puknos: Dense, thick, frequent

title: pulé: Gate, entrance
short_def: 1. a gate, i.e. the leaf or wing of a folding entrance{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: a gate, porchApparently a primary word; a gate, i.e. The leaf or wing of a folding entrance (literally or figuratively) -- gate.

title: pulón: Gate, gateway, entrance
short_def: 1. a gate-way, door-way of a building or city2. (by implication) a portal or vestibule
strongs_def: a vestibule, gatewayFrom pule; a gate-way, door-way of a building or city; by implication, a portal or vestibule -- gate, porch.

title: punthanomai: to inquire, to ask, to learn by inquiry
origin: [middle voice prolonged from a primary putho (which occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses)]
short_def: 1. to question, i.e. ascertain by inquiry2. (by implication) to learn (by casual intelligence){as a matter of information merely; and thus differing from G2065, which properly means a request as a favor; and from G154, which is strictly a demand for something due; as well as from G2212, which implies a search for something hidden; and from G1189, which involves the idea of urgent need}
strongs_def: ask, demand, inquire, understand. Middle voice prolonged from a primary putho (which occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses); to question, i.e. Ascertain by inquiry (as a matter of information merely; and thus differing from erotao, which properly means a request as a favor; and from aiteo, which is strictly a demand for something due; as well as from zeteo, which implies a search for something hidden; and from deomai, which involves the idea of urgent need); by implication, to learn (by casual intelligence) -- ask, demand, enquire, understand.
nas_def: to inquire, by impl. to learn
nasb: ask (1), asking (2), inquire (4), inquired (2), inquiring (1), learned (1).

title: pur: Fire
strongs_def: fiery, fire. A primary word; "fire" (literally or figuratively, specially, lightning) -- fiery, fire.

title: pura: Fire, burning
strongs_def: fire. From pur; a fire (concretely) -- fire.

title: purgos: Tower
strongs_def: tower. Apparently a primary word ("burgh"); a tower or castle -- tower.

title: puretos: Fever
strongs_def: fever. From puresso; inflamed, i.e. (by implication) feverish (as noun, fever) -- fever.

title: purinos: Fiery, of fire
strongs_def: fieryFrom pura; fiery, i.e. (by implication) flaming -- of fire.

title: puroó: To burn, to set on fire, to inflame

strongs_def: burn, be on fire, try. From pur; to kindle, i.e. (passively) to be ignited, glow (literally), be refined (by implication), or (figuratively) to be inflamed (with anger, grief, lust) -- burn, fiery, be on fire, try.

nasb: burn (1), burning (1), flaming (1), intense concern (1), made to glow (1), refined (1).

strongs_def: burning, trial. From puroo; ignition, i.e. (specially), smelting (figuratively, conflagration, calamity as a test) -- burning, trial.

origin: [another form of the base of G4458 (πώς - perhaps)]
short_def: 1. an enclitic particle of indefiniteness{used only in the comparative}

title: pólos: Axis, pole, or pivot

strongs_def: at any timeFrom po and pote; at any time, i.e. (with negative particle) at no time -- at any time, + never (...to any man), + yet, never man.

short_def: 1. to petrify2. (figuratively) to indurate (render stupid or callous)

strongs_def: callousness, blindness, hardness. From poroo; stupidity or callousness -- blindness, hardness.

title: pós: perhaps, somehow
origin: [adverb from the base of G4225 (πού - somewhere), an enclitic particle of indefiniteness of manner]

title: pós: how, what, why
origin: [adverb from the base of G4226 (ποῦ - where), an interrogative particle of manner]
short_def: 1. in what way?2. (sometimes indirect) how?3. (as exclamation) how much!{Occasionally unexpressed in English}
strongs_def: at all, somehowAdverb from the base of pou; an interrogative particle of manner; in what way? (sometimes the question is indirect, how?); also as exclamation, how much! -- how, after (by) what manner (means), that. (Occasionally unexpressed in English).

title: Rhaab: Rahab
strongs_def: Rahab. Of Hebrew origin (Rachab); Raab (i.e. Rachab), a Canaanitess -- Rahab. See also Rhachab.

title: rhabbi: Rabbi
origin: [of Hebrew origin (H7227 (רַב - Many) with pronominal suffix)]
short_def: 1. my master, i.e Rabbi, as an official title of honor
strongs_def: Master, Rabbi. Of Hebrew origin (rab with pronominal suffix); my master, i.e Rabbi, as an official title of honor -- Master, Rabbi.

title: rhabbouni: Teacher, Master

short_def: 1. my master, i.e Rabboni, as an official title of honor
strongs_def: Lord, Rabboni. Or rhabbouni (hrab-boo-nee') of Chaldee origin; corresponding to rhabbi -- Lord, Rabboni.

title: rhabdizó: To beat with a rod, to scourge
short_def: 1. to strike with a stick, i.e. bastinado
strongs_def: beat with rods. From rhabdos; to strike with a stick, i.e. Bastinado -- beat (with rods).

title: rhabdos: Rod, staff, scepter
short_def: 1. a stick or wand (as a cudgel, a cane or a baton of royalty)
strongs_def: rod, scepter, staff. From the base of rhapizo; a stick or wand (as a cudgel, a cane or a baton of royalty) -- rod, sceptre, staff.

title: rhabdouchos: Magistrate, Officer, Lictor
short_def: 1. a rod- (the Latin fasces) holder, i.e. a Roman lictor (constable or executioner)

title: Rhagau: Reu

title: rhadiourgéma: Mischief, wickedness, evil deed

strongs_def: lewdness. From a comparative of rhaidios (easy, i.e. Reckless) and ergon; easy-going behavior, i.e. (by extension) a crime -- lewdness.

title: rhadiourgia: Villainy, Mischief, Deceit

strongs_def: mischief. From the same as rhaidiourgema; recklessness, i.e. (by extension) malignity -- mischief.

title: rhaka: Fool; Empty-headed; Worthless

strongs_def: Raca. Of Chaldee origin (compare reyq); O empty one, i.e. Thou worthless (as a term of utter vilification) -- Raca.

title: rhakos: Rag, piece of cloth
strongs_def: cloth. From rhegnumi; a "rag," i.e. Piece of cloth -- cloth.

title: Rhama: Ramah
short_def: 1. Rama (i.e. Ramah), a place in Israel
strongs_def: Rama. Of Hebrew origin (Ramah); Rama (i.e. Ramah), a place in Palestine -- Rama.

title: rhantizó: To sprinkle
short_def: 1. to render besprinkled, i.e. asperse{ceremonially or figuratively}
strongs_def: sprinkle. From a derivative of rhaino (to sprinkle); to render besprinkled, i.e. Asperse (ceremonially or figuratively) -- sprinkle.

title: rhantismos: Sprinkling
strongs_def: sprinkling. From rhantizo; aspersion (ceremonially or figuratively) -- sprinkling.

title: rhapizó: To strike with a rod, to slap, to hit with the palm of the hand.
origin: [from a derivative of a primary rhepo "to let fall, to rap"]
kjv: smite (with the palm of the hand)
strongs_def: slap, smite with the palm of the hand. From a derivative of a primary rhepo (to let fall, "rap"); to slap -- smite (with the palm of the hand). Compare tupto.

title: rhapisma: Slap, blow, strike
kjv: (+ strike with the) palm of the hand, smite with the hand
strongs_def: a blow, slap in the faceFrom rhapizo; a slap -- (+ strike with the) palm of the hand, smite with the hand.

title: rhaphis: Needle
origin: [from a primary rhapto "to sew" (perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω - slapped) through the idea of puncturing)]
strongs_def: needle. From a primary rhapto (to sew; perhaps rather akin to the base of rhapizo through the idea of puncturing); a needle -- needle.

title: Rhachab: Rahab

title: strateia: warfare, military service, campaign
short_def: 1. military service2. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger)
strongs_def: warfare. From strateuomai; military service, i.e. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger) -- warfare.

title: strateuma: Army, troops, soldiers
short_def: 1. an armament2. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic)
strongs_def: army, soldier, man of war. From strateuomai; an armament, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic) -- army, soldier, man of war.
nas_def: an expedition, an army, a company of soldiers

title: strateuó: To wage war, to serve as a soldier, to engage in spiritual warfare
origin: [middle voice from the base of G4756 (στρατία - host)]
short_def: 1. to serve in a military campaign2. (figuratively) to execute the apostolate (with its arduous duties and functions), to contend with carnal inclinations
strongs_def: go to warMiddle voice from the base of stratia; to serve in a military campaign; figuratively, to execute the apostolate (with its arduous duties and functions), to contend with carnal inclinations -- soldier, (go to) war(-fare).
nas_def: to make war, hence to serve as a soldier
nasb: active service (1), fight (1), serves as a soldier (1), soldier in active service (1), soldiers (1), wage war (2), war (1).

title: stratégos: Commander, Magistrate, Captain
origin: [from the base of G4756 (στρατία - host) and G71 (ἄγω - brought) or G2233 (ἡγέομαι - regard)]
short_def: 1. a general2. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (praetor), the chief (prefect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens
strongs_def: captain, magistrate. From the base of stratia and ago or hegeomai; a general, i.e. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (praetor), the chief (praefect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens -- captain, magistrate.

title: stratia: Army, host
origin: [feminine of a derivative of stratos (an army, from the base of G4766 (στρώννυμι - furnished), as encamped)]
short_def: 1. camp-likeness, i.e. an army2. (figuratively) the angels, the celestial luminaries
strongs_def: army, hostFeminine of a derivative of stratos (an army; from the base of stronnumi, as encamped); camp-likeness, i.e. An army, i.e. (figuratively) the angels, the celestial luminaries -- host.

title: stratiótés: Soldier
origin: [from a presumed derivative of the same as G4756 (στρατία - host)]
short_def: 1. a camper-out, i.e. a (common) warrior{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: soldier. From a presumed derivative of the same as stratia; a camper-out, i.e. A (common) warrior (literally or figuratively) -- soldier.

title: stratologeó: To enlist soldiers, to recruit for military service
origin: [from a compound of the base of G4756 (στρατία - host) and G3004 (λέγω - said) (in its original sense)]
short_def: 1. to gather (or select) as a warrior, i.e. enlist in the army
strongs_def: enlistFrom a compound of the base of stratia and lego (in its original sense); to gather (or select) as a warrior, i.e. Enlist in the army -- choose to be a soldier.

title: stratia: Army, host
short_def: 1. a ruler of an army2. (specially), a Praetorian prefect
strongs_def: captain of the guard. From stratopedon and archo; a ruler of an army, i.e. (specially), a Praetorian praefect -- captain of the guard.

title: stratopedon: Camp, army camp, military encampment
origin: [from the base of G4756 (στρατία - host) and the same as G3977 (πεδινός - level)]
short_def: 1. a camping-ground2. (by implication) a body of troops
strongs_def: army. From the base of stratia and the same as pedinos; a camping-ground, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops -- army.
nas_def: a military camp, i.e. an army

title: strebloó: To twist, to distort
short_def: 1. to wrench2. (specially) to torture (by the rack)3. (figuratively) to pervert
strongs_def: wrest. From a derivative of strepho; to wrench, i.e. (specially), to torture (by the rack), but only figuratively, to pervert -- wrest.

title: strephó: To turn, to change, to convert
origin: [strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή - shifting)]
kjv: convert, turn (again, back again, self, self about)
short_def: 1. to turn (around)2. (figuratively) to turn back, return{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: turn, convertStrengthened from the base of trope; to twist, i.e. Turn quite around or reverse (literally or figuratively) -- convert, turn (again, back again, self, self about).
nasb: converted (2), returned (1), turn (3), turned (8), turned away (1), turned back (1), turning (5).

title: stréniaó: To live luxuriously, to revel, to indulge in luxury
origin: [from a presumed derivative of G4764 (στρήνος - sensuality)]
strongs_def: live luxuriously From a presumed derivative of strenos; to be luxurious -- live deliciously.

title: strénos: Luxury, revelry, indulgence
strongs_def: delicacy, luxuryAkin to stereos; a "straining", "strenuousness" or "strength", i.e. (figuratively) luxury (voluptuousness) -- delicacy.

title: strouthion: sparrow
strongs_def: sparrow. Diminutive of strouthos (a sparrow); a little sparrow -- sparrow.

title: strónnuó: to spread, to make a bed, to lay out
origin: [probably akin to G4731 (στερεός - firm) through the idea of positing]
short_def: 1. to "strew," i.e. spread (as a carpet or couch)
strongs_def: make bed, furnish, spread, strew. Or simpler stronnuo (strone-noo'-o), prolongation from a still simpler stroo (stro'-o), (used only as an alternate in certain tenses) (probably akin to stereos through the idea of positing); to "strew," i.e. Spread (as a carpet or couch) -- make bed, furnish, spread, strew.

title: stugétos: Hateful, detestable
origin: [from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary stugo "to hate"]
strongs_def: hateful. From a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary stugo (to hate); hated, i.e. Odious -- hateful.

title: stugnazó: To be gloomy, to look sullen, to be downcast
origin: [from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary stugo "to hate"]
short_def: 1. to render gloomy2. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech)
strongs_def: lower, be sad. From the same as stugnetos; to render gloomy, i.e. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech) -- lower, be sad.

title: stulos: Pillar
origin: [from stuo "to stiffen" (properly akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι - standing))]
strongs_def: pillar. From stuo (to stiffen; properly akin to the base of histemi); a post ("style"), i.e. (figuratively) support -- pillar.

title: Stóikos: Stoic
short_def: 1. a "Stoic" (as occupying a particular porch in Athens), i.e. adherent of a certain philosophy
strongs_def: a StoicFrom stoa; a "Stoic" (as occupying a particular porch in Athens), i.e. Adherent of a certin philosophy -- Stoick.

title: su: you
origin: [personal pronoun of the second person singular]
strongs_def: youThe person pronoun of the second person singular; thou -- thou. See also se, soi, sou; and for the plural humas, humeis, humin, humon.

title: suggeneia: Kindred, family, relatives
short_def: 1. relationship2. (concretely) relatives, kinsmen, kin (kinsfolk or kindred3. male and female)
strongs_def: kindred. From suggenes; relationship, i.e. (concretely) relatives -- kindred.

title: suggenes: Relative, kinsman, kindred
short_def: 1. a relative, a kinsman (by blood)2. (by extension) a fellow countryman3. (by hebrew analogy) a kinsman redeemer
strongs_def: relative, kinsman, cousinFrom sun and genos; a relative (by blood); by extension, a fellow countryman -- cousin, kin(-sfolk, -sman).

title: suggnómé: Pardon, Forgiveness, Understanding
origin: [from a compound of G4862 (σύν - along) and G1097 (γινώσκω - know)]
strongs_def: concession, permission. From a compound of sun and ginosko; fellow knowledge, i.e. Concession -- permission.

title: sugkathémai: To sit together, to sit with
short_def: 1. to seat oneself in company with
strongs_def: sit with. From sun and kathemai; to seat oneself in company with -- sit with.

title: sugkathizó: To sit together, to sit down with
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2523 (καθίζω - sat down)]
short_def: 1. to give (or take) a seat in company with
strongs_def: sit down together. From sun and kathizo; to give (or take) a seat in company with -- (make) sit (down) together.
nas_def: to make to sit together, to sit together

title: sugkakopatheó: To suffer together, to endure hardship with
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2553 (κακοπαθέω - endure hardship)]
short_def: 1. to suffer hardship in company with
strongs_def: suffer togetherFrom sun and kakopatheo; to suffer hardship in company with -- be partaker of afflictions.
nas_def: to bear evil treatment along with

title: sugkakoucheomai: To suffer together, to be mistreated together
short_def: 1. to maltreat in company with2. (passively) endure persecution together
strongs_def: suffer affliction with. From sun and kakoucheo; to maltreat in company with, i.e. (passively) endure persecution together -- suffer affliction with.

title: sugkaleó: to call together, to summon
strongs_def: call together. From sun and kaleo; to convoke -- call together.
nasb: called...together (2), called together (3), calls together (2), summoned (1).

title: sugkaluptó: To cover up, to conceal, to hide together
strongs_def: cover. From sun and kalupto; to conceal altogether -- cover.

title: sugkamptó: To bend together, to bow down
strongs_def: bow down. From sun and kampto; to bend together, i.e. (figuratively) to afflict -- bow down.

title: sugkatabainó: To go down together, to descend with
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2597 (καταβαίνω - come down)]
strongs_def: go down with. From sun and katabaino; to descend in company with -- go down with.

title: sugkatathesis: Agreement, consent
short_def: 1. a deposition (of sentiment) in company with2. (figuratively) accord with
strongs_def: agreement. From sugkatatithemai; a deposition (of sentiment) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) accord with -- agreement.
nas_def: a putting down together, i.e. agreement

title: sugkatatithémi: To agree with, to consent, to assent
origin: [middle from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2698 (κατατίθημι - do)]
short_def: 1. to deposit (one's vote or opinion) in company with2. (figuratively) to accord with
strongs_def: consent. Mid from sun and katatithemi; to deposit (one's vote or opinion) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to accord with -- consent.
nas_def: to deposit together, hence to agree with

title: sugkatapséphizomai: To count together with, to vote with, to be numbered with.
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and a compound of G2596 (κατά - according) and G5585 (ψηφίζω - calculate)]
short_def: 1. to count down in company with, i.e. enroll among
strongs_def: number with. From sun and a compound of kata and psephizo; to count down in company with, i.e. Enroll among -- number with.
nas_def: to condemn with, spec. to vote (one) a place among

title: sugkerannumi: To mix together, to blend, to unite
short_def: 1. to commingle2. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate
strongs_def: mix with, temper together. From sun and kerannumi; to commingle, i.e. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate -- mix with, temper together.
nas_def: to mix together, hence to agree with

title: sugkineó: To stir up together, to agitate, to incite
origin: [from G4682 (σπαράσσω - Throwing into convulsions) and G2795 (κινέω - move)]
short_def: 1. to move together2. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition)
strongs_def: stir up. From sparasso and kineo; to move together, i.e. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition) -- stir up.
nas_def: to move together, fig. to stir up

title: sugkleió: To shut up, enclose, confine
short_def: 1. to shut together, i.e. include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to
strongs_def: enclose, shut up. From sun and kleio; to shut together, i.e. Include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to -- conclude, inclose, shut up.

title: sugkléronomos: Co-heir, joint-heir
short_def: 1. a co-heir2. (by analogy) participant in common
strongs_def: joint heir, heir togetherFrom sun and kleronomos; a co-heir, i.e. (by analogy) participant in common -- fellow (joint)-heir, heir together, heir with.

title: sugkoinóneó: To share in, to participate with, to have fellowship with.
kjv: communicate (have fellowship) with, be partaker of
short_def: 1. to share in company with, i.e. co-participate in
strongs_def: fellowship, communicate with, be partaker of. From sun and koinoneo; to share in company with, i.e. Co-participate in -- communicate (have fellowship) with, be partaker of.

title: sugkoinónos: Partaker, partner, sharer, companion
strongs_def: partaker, companionFrom sun and koinonos; a co-participant -- companion, partake(-r, -r with).

title: sugkomizó: To carry together, to bring together, to gather up.
short_def: 1. to convey together, i.e. collect or bear away in company with others
strongs_def: carry, bearFrom sun and komizo; to convey together, i.e. Collect or bear away in company with others -- carry.
nas_def: to bring together, spec. to take up (a body for burial)

title: sugkrinó: To compare, to interpret, to join together
short_def: 1. to judge of one thing in connection with another, i.e. combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance)
strongs_def: compare, combine From sun and krino; to judge of one thing in connection with another, i.e. Combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance) -- compare among (with).

title: sugkuptó: To bend together, to be bent over, to stoop.
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2955 (κύπτω - stoop down)]
short_def: 1. to stoop altogether, i.e. be completely overcome by
strongs_def: bow together. From sun and kupto; to stoop altogether, i.e. Be completely overcome by -- bow together.

title: sugkuria: coincidence, chance, circumstance
origin: [from a comparative of G4862 (σύν - along) and kureo "to light or happen" (from the base of G2962 (κύριος - Lord))]
strongs_def: chance. From a comparative of sun and kureo (to light or happen; from the base of kurios); concurrence, i.e. Accident -- chance.

title: sugchairó: To rejoice with, to be glad with
strongs_def: rejoice with From sun and chairo; to sympathize in gladness, congratulate -- rejoice in (with).

title: sugcheó or sugchunnó: To confuse, to confound, to mix up, to throw into disorder.
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and cheo "to pour" or its alternate]
kjv: confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar
short_def: 1. to commingle promiscuously2. (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder, to perplex (the mind)
strongs_def: confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar. Or sugchuno (soong-khoo'-no) from sun and cheo (to pour) or its alternate; to commingle promiscuously, i.e. (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder, to perplex (the mind) -- confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar.
nas_def: to pour together, i.e. to confuse, throw into confusion

title: sugchraomai: To associate with, to have dealings with, to mix with.
short_def: 1. to use jointly2. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common
strongs_def: associate, have dealings with. From sun and chraomai; to use jointly, i.e. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common -- have dealings with.
nas_def: to use together with, fig. to associate with

title: sugchusis: Confusion, disorder, tumult
strongs_def: confusion. From sugcheo; commixture, i.e. (figuratively) riotous disturbance -- confusion.

title: suzaó: To live together, to live with
short_def: 1. to continue to live in common with, i.e. co-survive{literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: live with. From sun and zao; to continue to live in common with, i.e. Co-survive (literally or figuratively) -- live with.

title: suzeugnumi: To join together, to yoke together
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and the base of G2201 (ζεῦγος - pair)]
strongs_def: join together. From sun and the base of zeugos; to yoke together, i.e. (figuratively) conjoin (in marriage) -- join together.

title: suzéteó: To discuss, to dispute, to debate, to question
short_def: 1. to investigate jointly, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil
strongs_def: dispute, enquire, question, reason together. From sun and zeteo; to investigate jointly, i.e. Discuss, controvert, cavil -- dispute (with), enquire, question (with), reason (together).
nas_def: to examine together, hence to dispute
nasb: argue (1), argued (1), arguing (3), debated (1), discuss (1), discussing (3).

title: suzétésis: Discussion, debate, questioning, dispute
strongs_def: disputation, reasoning. From suzeteo; mutual questioning, i.e. Discussion -- disputation(-ting), reasoning.

title: suzététés: Debater, Disputer
strongs_def: disputer. From suzeteo; a disputant, i.e. Sophist -- disputer.

title: suzugos: Yoke-fellow, companion, partner
short_def: 1. yoked together, co-yoked2. (figuratively, as noun) a colleague3. (a proper name) Syzygus, a Christian
strongs_def: yokefellow. From suzeugnumi; co-yoked, i.e. (figuratively) as noun, a colleague; probably rather as a proper name; Syzygus, a Christian -- yokefellow.

title: suzóopoieó: To make alive together, to quicken together
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G2227 (ζωοποιέω - gives life)]
strongs_def: make alive together with. From sun and zoopoieo; to reanimate conjointly with (figuratively) -- quicken together with.

title: sukaminos: Sycamine tree
origin: [of Hebrew origin (H8256 (שָׁקָם שִׁקמָה - sycamore trees)) in imitation of G4809 (συκομωραία - sycamore tree)]
strongs_def: sycamine tree, mulberry treeOf Hebrew origin (shaqam) in imitation of sukomoraia; a sycamore-fig tree -- sycamine tree.

title: suké: Fig tree
strongs_def: fig tree. From sukon; a fig-tree -- fig tree.

title: sukomorea: Sycamore-fig tree
strongs_def: sycamore tree. From sukon and moron (the mulberry); the "sycamore"-fig tree -- sycamore tree. Compare sukaminos.

title: sukon: Fig
strongs_def: fig. Apparently a primary word; a fig -- fig.

title: sukophanteó: To accuse falsely, to extort, to defraud
origin: [from a compound of G4810 (σῦκον - figs) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω - appeared)]
kjv: accuse falsely, take by false accusation
short_def: 1. to be a fig-informer (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant"2. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort)
strongs_def: accuse falsely, take by false accusation. From a compound of sukon and a derivative of phaino; to be a fig-informer (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant", i.e. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort) -- accuse falsely, take by false accusation.

title: sulagógeó: To carry off as spoil, to lead away as booty, to plunder.
origin: [from the base of G4813 (συλάω - robbed) and (the reduplicated form of) G71 (ἄγω - brought)]
short_def: 1. to lead away as booty2. (figuratively) seduce
strongs_def: take spoils. From the base of sulao and (the reduplicated form of) ago; to lead away as booty, i.e. (figuratively) seduce -- spoil.

title: sulaó: To despoil, to plunder, to strip
origin: [from a derivative of sullo "to strip" (probably akin to G138 (αἱρέομαι - choose))]
strongs_def: rob. From a derivative of sullo (to strip; probably akin to haireomai; compare skulon); to despoil -- rob.

title: sullaleó: To converse, to speak together, to talk with
strongs_def: confer, talk with, communeFrom sun and laleo; to talk together, i.e. Converse -- commune (confer, talk) with, speak among.

title: sullambanó: To seize, to apprehend, to conceive, to assist
short_def: 1. to clasp, i.e. seize (arrest, capture)2. (specially) to conceive3. (by implication) to aid{(specially) "conceive" literally or figuratively}
strongs_def: catch, conceive, help, take. From sun and lambano; to clasp, i.e. Seize (arrest, capture); specially, to conceive (literally or figuratively); by implication, to aid -- catch, conceive, help, take.
nas_def: to collect, i.e. to take, by impl. to take part with, spec. to conceive
nasb: arrest (3), arrested (4), became pregnant (1), conceive (1), conceived (3), help (2), seized (1), taken (1).

title: sullegó: To gather, to collect
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and G3004 (λέγω - said) in its original sense]
strongs_def: gather together From sun and lego in its original sense; to collect -- gather (together, up).

title: sullogizomai: To reason together, to consider, to deliberate.
short_def: 1. to reckon together (with oneself), i.e. deliberate
strongs_def: reason with. From sun and logizomai; to reckon together (with oneself), i.e. Deliberate -- reason with.
nas_def: to reckon, to compute, i.e. to reason

title: sullupeó: To grieve with, to be sorrowful with, to sympathize in grief
short_def: 1. to afflict jointly2. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone
strongs_def: be grieved. From sun and lupeo; to afflict jointly, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone -- be grieved.
nas_def: to be moved to grief with (pass.)

title: sumbainó: To happen, to occur, to come together
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and the base of G939 (βάσις - feet)]
short_def: 1. to walk together2. (figuratively) to coincide together, i.e. take place
strongs_def: befall, happen unto. From sun and the base of basis; to walk (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. Concur (take place) -- be(-fall), happen (unto).
nas_def: to come together, i.e. (of events) to come to pass
nasb: came (1), happen (1), happened (3), happening (1), taken place (1).

title: sumballó: To consider, to ponder, to confer, to meet, to encounter
kjv: confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder
short_def: 1. to combine2. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute3. (mentally) to consider4. (by implication) to aid5. (personally) to join, attack
strongs_def: discuss, confer, encounterFrom sun and ballo; to combine, i.e. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute, (mentally) to consider, (by implication) to aid, (personally) to join, attack -- confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder.
nas_def: to throw together, hence to discuss, consider, meet with
nasb: confer (1), conversing (1), helped (1), meet (1), met (1), pondering (1).

title: sumbasileuó: To reign together, to co-reign
strongs_def: reign with. From sun and basileuo; to be co-regent (figuratively) -- reign with.

title: sumbibazó: To unite, to bring together, to instruct, to prove
origin: [from G4862 (σύν - along) and bibazo "to force" (causative (by reduplication) of the base of G939 (βάσις - feet))]
kjv: compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove
short_def: 1. to drive together, i.e. unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach
strongs_def: to hold together, to instructFrom sun and bibazo (to force; causative (by reduplication) of the base of basis); to drive together, i.e. Unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach -- compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove.
nas_def: to join together, hence to consider, by ext. to teach
nasb: concluded (1), concluding (1), held (1), held together (1), instruct (1), knit together (1), proving (1), together* (1).

title: sumbouleuó: To advise, to counsel, to deliberate
short_def: 1. to give (or take) advice jointly, i.e. recommend, deliberate or determine
strongs_def: deliberate, plot, adviseFrom sun and bouleuo; to give (or take) advice jointly, i.e. Recommend, deliberate or determine -- consult, (give, take) counsel (together).

title: sumboulion: Council, counsel, plan, advice
origin: [neuter of a presumed derivative of G4825 (σύμβουλος - counselor)]
short_def: 1. advisement2. (specially) a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court
strongs_def: consultation, counsel, council. Neuter of a presumed derivative of sumboulos; advisement; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. The provincial assessors or lay-court -- consultation, counsel, council.
nasb: conferred* (2), conspired* (1), conspiring (1), consultation (1), consulted* (1), council (1), plotted together (1), together (2).

title: sumboulos: Counselor, Advisor
strongs_def: counselor. From sun and boule; a consultor, i.e. Adviser -- counsellor.

title: Sumeón: Simeon
short_def: 1. Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites
strongs_def: Simeon, Simon. From the same as Simon; Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites -- Simeon, Simon.
nas_def: Symeon, Simeon, the name of several Isr., also a tribe of Isr.

title: summathétés: Fellow disciple, fellow student
origin: [from a compound of G4862 (σύν - along) and G3129 (μανθάνω - learn)]
strongs_def: fellow disciple. From a compound of sun and manthano; a co-learner (of Christianity) -- fellow disciple.

title: summartureó: To bear witness with, to testify together
short_def: 1. to testify jointly, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence
strongs_def: testify unto, bear witnessFrom sun and martureo; to testify jointly, i.e. Corroborate by (concurrent) evidence -- testify unto, (also) bear witness (with).
nas_def: to testify or bear witness with

title: summerizó: To reckon together, to calculate, to sum up

(intransitively) to happen (as if meeting with){(intransitively) in the latter application only impersonal (with G1487), i.e. perchance; or (present participle) as adjective, usual (as if commonly met with, with G3756, extraordinary), neuter (as adverb) perhaps; or (with another verb) as adverb, by accident (as it were)}

(figuratively) to offend (the conscience){differing from G3817 and G3960, which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 with the palm; as well as from G5177, an accidental collision}

title: huper: for, on behalf of, above, beyond, more than
kjv: (+ exceeding, abundantly) above, in (on) behalf of, beyond, by, + very chiefest, concerning, exceeding (above, -ly), for, + very highly, more (than), of, over, on the part of, for sake of, in stead, than, to(-ward), very In the comparative, it retains many of the above applications

In the comparative, it retains many of the above applications.

title: chóra: Land, region, country, field
